Job Overview:

We are seeking a highly skilled Structural Engineer / Revit Modeler to join our team. This role focuses on designing and detailing multi-level storage buildings, which require a unique structural approach due to their heavy load-bearing systems and specialized construction methods.

The ideal candidate is not only a strong structural engineer but also a super expert in Revit modeling, capable of creating precise, complex models with minimal supervision.



Key Responsibilities:

Design and model heavy, multi-level storage buildings using Revit.

Perform structural analysis and detailing specific to high-load industrial and storage facilities.

Develop accurate construction documents and shop drawings.

Collaborate with the engineering and design team to ensure models meet structural and architectural requirements.

Maintain accuracy and consistency across all Revit models and related documentation.

Coordinate with project managers and other disciplines to ensure seamless project delivery.



Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Structural Engineering, Civil Engineering, or related field.

Industrial and commercial engineering experience preferred, particularly with heavy load-bearing structures.

Proven expertise as a Revit Modeler, with advanced knowledge of large-scale modeling.

Strong understanding of structural systems, especially for multi-level and heavy storage buildings.

Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills.

Strong communication and collaboration abilities in a remote team environment.



Preferred Skills:

Experience with Steel Structures, Concrete Structures, Masonry Construction, Light and Gauge Steel Structures

Familiarity with BIM coordination and clash detection.

Knowledge of U.S. building codes and standards is a plus.

Knowledge of ETABS and SAFE is a plus.

title: Entry-Level Cold Caller / Opener / Lead Generator – No Experience Needed!
description:
Position: Remote Cold Caller / Opener / Lead Generator – Start Your Sales Career Today

Company: Catalyst– Fast-Growing Marketing & Funding Company (US-based)

Location: 100% Remote (Philippines – perfect for home-based workers)

Type: Full-Time or Part-Time ( flexible shifts to match US hours)

Pay Structure (Real Earnings Potential):



Base Pay: $1.00 per hour (steady while you learn)

App Bonus: $2.00–$3.00 per qualified booked appointment (your main money-maker!)

Funding Bonus: Extra cash bonuses when your leads close and get funded (top performers add hundreds or even $1,000s/month)



What This Job Is About

No cold calling experience? No problem! We're a dynamic marketing and funding company. You'll be our frontline opener: dialing individuals who might need fast funding for inventory, payroll, marketing, or growth. Use our proven scripts, overcome simple objections, qualify them quickly, and book them for our expert closers. It's high-volume outbound calling — the more you dial and book, the more you earn!

This is perfect if you're:



New to sales but willing to learn and grind

A fast learner with good English and a positive attitude

Looking to build real sales skills in finance/marketing

Motivated by bonuses (many of our team started at entry-level and now earn big!)



Your Daily Wins



Make outbound cold calls (100–200+ per shift) using our easy dialer system

Follow simple scripts to pitch MCA cash advances and credit lines

Chat with business owners (restaurants, shops, contractors, online sellers, etc.)

Ask quick questions to qualify (revenue, needs, etc.)

Book qualified appointments/transfers for our funding team

Log calls and results

Hit fun daily targets to unlock bonuses



Requirements – Super Beginner-Friendly



No experience necessary – we'll train you step-by-step!

Clear English speaking (phone-friendly accent is great)

Reliable high-speed internet, quiet workspace, headset, and laptop/PC

Positive mindset — rejection is normal, persistence wins here

Available for US time zone shifts (e.g., PH morning/evening overlap with EST/PST)

Honest, hardworking, and excited to earn more through performance



Why You'll Love It Here



No experience? No issue — we hire attitude over resume

Work from home in your PJs — total freedom

Weekly or bi-weekly payouts so you see money fast

Uncapped bonuses — hustle hard and your paycheck grows fast

Real career path: Start as a cold caller ? become an opener ? move to closer roles

Supportive team with scripts, training calls, and tips from pros

Help US businesses grow while building your own income



Ready to start dialing and stacking bonuses? Apply today!

Send your resume (or just tell us about yourself), a quick 20–30 second voice recording introducing yourself, and why you're excited about sales/funding.
 to [your hiring
] or apply via [link/DM].

Let's get you booked and paid — spots fill up quick for motivated starters!

title: Virtual Assistant – Inbound Call Specialist & Intake Coordinator
description:
Virtual Assistant – Inbound Call Specialist & Intake Coordinator

Robland Home Health Care & Aspen FMS



Position: Virtual Assistant – Phone & Intake Support

Employer: Robland Home Health Care / Aspen FMS

Work Hours: Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Central Standard Time (CST)

Work Type: Remote / Virtual

Role Focus: Inbound phone support, client & employee intake, call transfers

Language Requirement: Fluent, clear, professional English (spoken & written)



About Us

Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS are dedicated to providing high-quality in-home care services and financial management support to the individuals and families we serve. Our clients and employees are the heart of what we do, and every phone call is an opportunity to deliver a caring, professional, and seamless experience. We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to be the friendly, dependable voice on the other end of the line.

Role Overview

The Virtual Assistant will serve as the primary point of contact for all incoming phone calls during business hours. This is a phone-centric role — your core responsibility will be answering calls, handling client and employee concerns with professionalism and empathy, conducting initial intake for new clients and prospective employees, and transferring calls to the appropriate tea
mber when needed. This role is critical to our operations and requires someone who is calm under pressure, well-spoken, organized, and genuinely caring toward the people they speak with.

Key Responsibilities

Inbound Call Handling (Primary Duty)

• Answer all incoming calls promptly, professionally, and with a warm, welcoming tone.

• Serve as the first voice representing Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS to clients, families, employees, and prospects.

• Listen actively to callers, identify the purpose of the call, and route accordingly.

• Maintain composure and professionalism on every call, including emotionally sensitive or difficult conversations.

• Keep call volume moving efficiently without making callers feel rushed.

Client Intake

• Conduct initial intake calls with new and prospective clients, collecting accurate personal, contact, and service-related information.

• Explain services clearly and answer basic questions about what Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS offer.

• Document all intake information correctly in our systems for handoff to the appropriate team.

• Identify urgent needs and escalate promptly to the correct department or supervisor.

Employee Intake

• Handle inbound calls from prospective employees and caregivers interested in joining our team.

• Collect initial application information, answer basic hiring-process questions, and route qualified leads to the hiring coordinator or HR.

• Provide clear, accurate information about next steps in the application process.

Call Transfers & Routing

• Transfer calls accurately to the correct department, tea
mber, or supervisor.

• Announce transfers professionally, briefing the receiving party on the caller and reason when appropriate.

• Take detailed, accurate messages when a call cannot be connected, and ensure timely follow-up delivery.

• Maintain and follow an updated internal directory and call-routing guide.

Client & Employee Concerns

• Address and de-escalate client complaints and employee concerns with patience, empathy, and professionalism.

• Log concerns accurately and forward them to the correct supervisor or department for resolution.

• Follow up when required to ensure the caller feels heard and the issue is being handled.

• Maintain strict confidentiality regarding all client, employee, and company information.

Required Qualifications

• Fluent English: Near-native or native-level spoken English with a clear, neutral accent and strong listening comprehension. Written English must also be professional and error-free.

• Phone Presence: A warm, confident, professional phone voice. Able to project calm and competence on every call.

• Availability: Must be reliably available Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST, with no regular scheduling conflicts during these hours.

• Experience: At least 1–2 years of experience in a call center, receptionist, customer service, virtual assistant, or intake coordinator role. Healthcare or home care experience is a strong plus.

• Technology: Comfortable with VoIP phone systems, CRM software, Microsoft Office / Google Workspace,
, and basic data entry. Able to learn new systems quickly.

• Home Office Setup: Quiet, distraction-free workspace; reliable high-speed internet; a working computer; a good-quality USB or wired headset; and a dependable backup power or internet plan.

• Soft Skills: Strong active listening, empathy, patience, multitasking, attention to detail, and the ability to stay organized while managing back-to-back calls.

• Professionalism: Discretion, confidentiality, and a customer-first mindset in every interaction.

Preferred (Nice-to-Have)

• Previous experience working with home health care, home care, assisted living, or FMS (Financial Management Services) agencies.

• Familiarity with HIPAA and basic healthcare privacy practices.

• Bilingual (English/Spanish) ability is a plus but not required.

• Prior remote work experience with a proven track record of reliability.

What Success Looks Like

• Calls are answered quickly (within the first few rings) during all business hours.

• Callers consistently describe the experience as friendly, professional, and helpful.

• Intake information is captured accurately and delivered to the right team without delay.

• Transfers land in the right place, with the right context, every time.

• Client and employee concerns are handled with care and nothing slips through the cracks.

Compensation & Schedule

Schedule: Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST (40 hours per week)

Location: Fully remote

Pay: [Rate to be discussed based on experience]

Start Date: As soon as possible for the right candidate

How to Apply

To apply, please submit the following:

• An updated resume or CV.

• A short cover letter or note (3–5 sentences) explaining why you’re a good fit for this phone-focused role.

• A voice sample — either a 30–60 second recording of you introducing yourself and answering a sample call in English, or the ability to complete a brief phone screen.

• Your available start date and confirmation that you can commit to the full 9-to-5 CST schedule, Monday through Friday.

Only candidates with strong English fluency and reliable availability during the stated hours will be considered. We’re looking for someone who wants to be a long-term part of our team — if that’s you, we’d love to hear from you.



Robland Home Health Care  •  Aspen FMS

OneZero Software Inc. is an innovative Alberta-based tech company developing advanced case-management and ERP-integrated solutions for enterprise and institutional environments. Built by experienced entrepreneurs with a strong background in construction, real estate, and technology, OneZero is scaling rapidly and seeking a high-performing Business Development leader to drive growth across Canada and the U.S.

This role is fully remote, but you will work closely with our Edmonton-based leadership team. The ideal candidate has a strong background in software / SaaS sales, enterprise business development, and building long-term client relationships.

CRM experience (HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar) required.

title: Position: Dedicated Biller – Home Care & Financial Management Services
description:
Experienced Home Health Care Biller

Robland Home Health Care & Aspen FMS



Position: Dedicated Biller – Home Health Care & FMS

Employer: Robland Home Health Care / Aspen FMS

Experience Required: Must be already trained and experienced in home health care billing

Work Hours: Monday through Friday, business hours (Central Time)

Work Type: Remote / Virtual

Role Focus: Full-cycle billing under MnDHS guidelines, Medical Assistance (MA), waivered services, and MCOs



About Us

Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS provide in-home care and Financial Management Services (FMS) to Minnesotans enrolled in programs administered through the Minnesota Department of Human Services (MnDHS). Accurate, on-time billing is how our caregivers get paid and how the company stays healthy. We’re hiring an experienced biller to take full ownership of this function.

Role Overview

This is not an entry-level or trainee role. We are looking for a biller who already knows home health care billing inside and out — specifically billing under MnDHS guidelines, Medical Assistance (MA), waivered services, and managed care organizations (MCOs). You should be able to hit the ground running: submitting claims in MN–ITS, verifying authorizations, reconciling remittance advices, and working denials without step-by-step hand-holding.

You will own the full billing cycle for both Robland Home Health Care and Aspen FMS. This role reports directly to leadership and works closely with intake, scheduling, and payroll. Because billing accuracy drives revenue and compliance, this is one of the most trusted seats in the company.

Key Responsibilities

Claim Submission

• Submit clean claims to MN–ITS, MA, waivered services, and applicable MCOs on a consistent weekly / bi-weekly cycle.

• Verify that each claim matches the service authorization, documented hours, correct service codes and modifiers, and participant eligibility for the date(s) of service.

• Stay ahead of MnDHS timely-filing deadlines — no missed windows.

• Maintain organized, auditable billing records for every claim submitted.

Authorization & Eligibility

• Verify participant eligibility and active service authorizations before billing.

• Track authorization units, start/end dates, and remaining balances; flag authorizations about to expire or run out.

• Coordinate with intake to ensure new authorizations are captured and entered before services are billed.

Denials, Rejections & AR Follow-up

• Read and reconcile remittance advices (RAs / 835s); identify denials, rejections, and underpayments.

• Diagnose root causes — auth mismatches, documentation gaps, coding issues, eligibility lapses — correct and resubmit within filing windows.

• Maintain a denial log, surface trends, and fix recurring issues at the source rather than re-billing the same problem.

• Keep accounts receivable clean and well-aged; nothing slips past recoverable windows.

Compliance & Documentation

• Ensure all billed services are supported by compliant documentation (timesheets, visit notes, EVV records, care plans) before submission.

• Follow MnDHS billing guidelines, MA policies, and FMS program rules exactly as written.

• Maintain strict HIPAA compliance and confidentiality on all participant, employee, and payer information.

• Pull records quickly for internal or external audits.

Reporting & Communication

• Provide weekly billing reports to leadership: claims submitted, paid, pending, denied, and aging AR.

• Flag revenue risks early — missed windows, repeated denials, documentation gaps, auth issues.

• Communicate clearly with intake, scheduling, and payroll when billing issues trace back to their workflows.

• Respond to MnDHS, MCO, and payer inquiries professionally and on time.

Required Qualifications

• Home Health Care Billing Experience: 2+ years of hands-on billing experience in home health care, home care, PCA, CFSS, waivered services, or a similar Minnesota-based care setting. This is non-negotiable — applicants without direct home care billing experience will not be considered.

• MnDHS / MN–ITS Proficiency: Direct experience submitting claims through MN–ITS under MnDHS guidelines. You know the system, the service codes, and the rules without needing to be taught them.

• Payer Knowledge: Working knowledge of Medical Assistance (MA), waiver programs, and Minnesota managed care organizations (UCare, Blue Plus, Medica, HealthPartners, etc.).

• Denial Management: Proven ability to read 835s / remittance advices, diagnose denials, and rework claims within timely-filing windows.

• EVV Familiarity: Understands Electronic Visit Verification and how EVV data flows into claims.

• Detail-Oriented: Obsessive accuracy with numbers, codes, dates, and documentation. Small errors create large problems in this role.

• Self-Directed: Able to own the billing function independently, set your own cadence, and hit deadlines without being managed minute-to-minute.

• Technology: Comfortable with billing / EHR software, payer portals, Excel / Google Sheets, and payroll coordination.

• Reliability & Discretion: Consistent, on-time performance and full confidentiality on sensitive participant, payroll, and financial information.

• Home Office Setup: Quiet, private workspace; reliable high-speed internet; a working computer; and a dependable backup plan.

Preferred (Nice-to-Have)

• Prior experience billing for an FMS (Financial Management Services) agency.

• CPB, CPC, or other billing / coding certifications.

• Experience supervising or training other billers.

• Experience cleaning up a billing backlog or rebuilding a billing process from the ground up.

What Success Looks Like

• Clean claims go out on time, every cycle — zero missed timely-filing deadlines.

• Denial rate is low and trending down; recurring denial reasons get fixed at the source.

• AR stays healthy and well-aged; nothing quietly slips past recoverable windows.

• Leadership has clear weekly visibility into billing performance and revenue risk.

• Audits are handled calmly because the documentation trail is already clean.

Compensation & Schedule

Schedule: Monday – Friday, standard business hours (Central Time)

Location: Fully remote

Pay: [Rate to be discussed based on experience]

Start Date: As soon as possible for the right candidate

How to Apply

To apply, please submit the following:

• An updated resume or CV specifically highlighting your home health care billing experience — agencies, years, programs billed (MA, waivers, PCA, CFSS, etc.), and systems used (MN–ITS, EHRs, clearinghouses).

• A short cover letter (3–5 sentences) describing the billing setup you’ve owned before and what you’re proud of in your work — clean claim rate, denial turnaround, backlog cleanup, etc.

• Any relevant certifications (CPB, CPC, etc.).

• Your available start date and confirmation that you can commit to a consistent weekday schedule.

Please do not apply if you do not already have direct home health care billing experience. We’re looking for someone who can walk in, plug in, and run the billing function with confidence from day one.



Robland Home Health Care  •  Aspen FMS

description:

YOU WILL BE ASKED TO SHARE THE RAW PROJECT FILE FROM AE AND PREMIER or PHOTOSHOP AND ILLUSTRATOR FROM SELECTED VIDEOS AND GRAPHIC DESIGNS OUT OF YOUR CURRICULUM TO VALIDATE YOUR PREVIOUS WORKS

Key Responsibilities



Video Editing & Motion Graphics



Edit short-form content (Reels, TikToks, YouTube Shorts) – minimum 3-4 x 1-minute videos per week

Manage and organise project files, proxies, and assets in a clean, repeatable structure



(Nice to Have) Social Media & Strategy

Understand best practices for YouTube, Instagram,
, TikTok (hooks, pacing, captions, aspect ratios)



Coordinate with the social media manager on content ideas, hooks, and titles



Basic knowledge of YouTube SEO (titles, descriptions, tags, thumbnails that drive CTR)

5+ years professional experience as a video editor / motion graphics designer



Expert-level skills in:



Adobe Premiere Pro



Adobe After Effects



Adobe Illustrator



Adobe Photoshop



Adobe Lightroom



Strong portfolio showing:



Short-form social content



Long-form YouTube videos



Thumbnails and graphic design work



(Bonus) real estate or property videos



Comfortable working remote, managing deadlines, and handling feedback quickly



Strong English communication (written & spoken)



Stable internet connection and a reliable computer capable of high-res video editing



Nice-to-Have (Desired, Not Required)



Experience managing or supporting social media accounts (YouTube, Instagram,
, TikTok)



Experience with real estate, education, or online course content

?

?

?

?

?

This role is for designers who understand real packaging production—not just digital graphics. If you do not have dieline experience, do not apply.

title: Operations Coordinator
description:
About LineGear

LineGear supplies PPE, uniforms, and equipment to fire departments and wildland firefighters across California. Our customers include municipal fire departments, state agencies, and individual firefighters.

LineGear serves government fire agencies and operates in a compliance-sensitive procurement environment where accuracy in PO documentation and vendor records is critical.

This role is best suited for candidates who enjoy operations, organization, and managing order workflows.



Role Overview

The Operations Coordinator owns the operational workflow of orders and vendor purchasing, ensuring orders move efficiently through Quote  >  Sales Order  >  Purchase Order.

This role supports the sales team by ensuring orders are processed accurately, vendors are coordinated, and operational systems remain organized and audit-ready. The position is primarily internal operations and does not require phone support.



Key Responsibilities

Sales Order Processing

Manage incoming customer orders and ensure they are processed correctly, with same-day entry as the target SLA. Responsibilities include:

- Creating and updating sales orders

- Verifying order quantities, details, and inventory availability

- Identifying items requiring purchase orders



Uniform Order Processing

Monitor the inbox 
 include:

- Reviewing incoming uniform orders and entering them into the uniform tracking spreadsheet

- Verifying quantities, checking inventory availability

- Creating purchase orders when items are not in stock

- Tracking vendor shipments through to fulfillment



Purchase Order Management

Create and send purchase orders to vendors in accordance with the authority guidelines. 

Additional responsibilities include:

- Reviewing sales orders for inventory shortages before issuing POs

- Confirming vendor receipt of each PO

- Recording expected ship dates and tracking numbers

- Monitoring backorders and flagging delays

- Purchase orders should generally be created only against confirmed sales orders, unless instructed otherwise by management.



Vendor Coordination

Communicate with vendors regarding order status and shipments. Vendor follow-up should occur within 3 business days of any open item. Tasks include:

- Confirming PO receipt and requesting shipment updates

- Recording tracking numbers and monitoring vendor lead times

- Escalating vendor delays to the manager via Slack, Teams, or daily
 update



Vendor File & Data Management

Maintain accurate vendor information across company systems, including:

- Vendor contact lists and pricing lists

- Product catalogs and vendor lead time notes

- Vendor information should be organized within QuickBooks vendor records, shared Google Drive folders, and internal pricing spreadsheets.



Order Status Research

Assist the internal team with order status inquiries. Tasks include:

- Checking inventory availability and verifying vendor shipment status

- Confirming expected delivery dates and providing internal updates to the team



Product & Pricing Data Maintenance

Maintain internal product data to support quoting and purchasing:

- Updating vendor pricing, maintaining SKU data, organizing product information

- Tracking vendor lead times and flagging changes



SOP Documentation

Create and maintain written standard operating procedures (SOPs) for all recurring tasks within this role. Responsibilities include:

- Documenting step-by-step workflows for order entry, PO issuance, vendor follow-up, and uniform order processing

- Updating SOPs when processes change

- Maintaining SOPs in a shared Google Drive folder accessible to the manager

- SOP documentation ensures operational continuity and is a standing expectation of the role, not a one-time task.



Tools Used

The Operations Coordinator will work primarily in:

QuickBooks Desktop (accessed via Remote Desktop — see required skills)

Google Sheets and Google Drive

Shared
 inboxes

Slack and/or Microsoft Teams



Work Schedule

Part-time position, at least 20 hours per week, overlap with U.S. Pacific Time business hours.



Location                       Hours

Philippines                   Approximately 12:00 AM – 8:00 AM PHT

California (PT)              Approximately 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM PT



Exact hours may vary slightly depending on operational needs.



Required Skills

QuickBooks Desktop experience — required. Candidates with QuickBooks Online experience only will need to demonstrate ability to learn QBD quickly. Access is provided via Remote Desktop; a stable, high-speed internet connection is required.

Strong written English

Excellent attention to detail

Strong spreadsheet skills (Excel or Google Sheets)

Ability to follow and document structured processes

Ability to manage multiple tasks and open orders simultaneously



Preferred Experience

Candidates with experience in the following areas are preferred:

Operations coordination, procurement, or purchasing

Logistics support or order management

Inventory tracking in a distribution or product-based business

B2B or government procurement environments



Compensation & Reporting

Starting compensation: $400/month

This role reports directly to Owner



Application Instructions

To confirm that you carefully read this job description, please include the word:



FIRELINE

in the first sentence of your application. Applications without this word may not be reviewed.



Send your application to 
 and answer the following questions in your
 and attach a resume for review.



1. Why are you the perfect fit for this role? Please include any examples of your previous work or projects that demonstrate your expertise.



2. Considering the diverse responsibilities of this role, which parts are you most comfortable or excited with? Which parts will you still need to master?



3. Have you worked for foreign employers before? What specific days and hours have you worked in the past?

title: Data Operations Associate
description:
PLEASE APPLY ONLY IF YOU HAVE AT LEAST ONE YEAR OF EXPERIENCE WORKING WITH EZLynx in the insurance industry.  Bonus if you have experience in Monday.com (CRM)



COMPANY OVERVIEW: 

With over 25 years in business, Premier Group Insurance is one of the largest and fastest growing Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance agencies in the United States. Insurance Journal ranks Premier in the Top 12 in Personal Lines out of an independent agency universe of nearly 40,000 agencies, and #38 in total P&C.  We are a team of dedicated professionals with a singular focus:  to be the best in the industry for our agency owners and the staff who support them.  We believe that a best-in-class insurance network should provide four key elements to insurance entrepreneurs:



Role and Responsibilities - Policy Download Matching & Enrollment

• Manage and reconcile policy downloads from insurance carriers, ensuring accurate matching to AMS client records. We use EZLynx as their primary Agency Management Systems (AMS)

• Investigate and resolve unmatched or incorrect policy data leveraging AMS and CRM tool. We use 
 as their primary CRM tool

• Collaborate with agents to troubleshoot download issues and provide operational support

• Enroll and onboard agencies for new carrier download connections, ensuring proper setup, maintenance and activation

• New insurance carrier setup

• Coordinate with Personal Lines, Commercial Lines, Commissions & Accounting, and Licensing departments to identify and resolve data discrepancies

• Track data discrepancies and recurring issues, reporting insights and recommending process improvements

• Update management on Data Trends & Analysis of mis-matches and reasons

• Book of Business data pull - download data from EZLynx into Excel format, create pivot tables and visualization for management



ABILITY TO:

• Work effectively in a collaborative team environment

• Learn multiple insurance carrier systems, reporting platforms

• Be comfortable communicating with insurance carriers and agent in resolving policy un-matches and carrier setup issues

• Think outside of the box and develop tools for Independent Agents

• Communicate clearly and effectively, both orally and in writing

• Be comfortable communicating with vendors (EZLynx, Carriers, etc.)

• Prepare and present clear and concise reports to variety of audiences

• Learn and become very comfortable with AMS (EZLynx) and CRM (
) tools

• Maintain confidentiality

• Plan and devise meetings and work plans

MINIMUM JOB REQUIREMENTS

title: People & Culture Leader
description:
If you run HR by waiting to be told what to do, this is not for you. We need someone who walks into ambiguity and builds order.



About the Role

Aureus Financial is a multi-division financial services and education group serving seven and eight-figure business owners across Australia. We have a team of 65-plus people across education, wealth advice, accounting, mortgage broking, and property. We move fast, hold high standards, and we are scaling.



We have split our HR function into internal and external. This role owns internal HR completely. Within 30 to 60 days of you starting, you will hire a People & Culture Coordinator who handles execution. Your job is to build the system, lead that person, and partner with our department heads to embed performance culture across the business.



Performance management at Aureus is a collaboration. You design the framework and the cadence. Department heads run the conversations with their teams. You hold them accountable to the schedule and the standard. This is the model — you need to be able to build it and make it stick without being heavy-handed.



Why This Role Is Worth Your Time

You will own the entire internal HR function — full design authority, no one telling you how to structure it.

You will hire and lead your own direct report within 60 days — this is a people leadership role, not a solo operator role.

Direct access to the CEO and Group Operations Leader from day one.

You will build the performance infrastructure for a growing business and get to see it work at scale.

PHP 100,
,000 per month, based on experience + performance bonus



What You Will Own

Pillar 1 — Team Leadership & HR Systems

Hire and lead a People & Culture Coordinator within your first 60 days

Design every HR process so it is a documented, repeatable system your Coordinator can run

Own compliance across all HR platforms and employment documentation



Pillar 2 — Talent Acquisition & Performance Framework

Accountable for all hiring outcomes — your Coordinator handles execution, you set the standard

Build the monthly performance review framework and partner with department heads to run it — they own the conversations, you own the system

Design KPI scorecards for every role and train department heads to use them



Pillar 3 — Culture & Strategic Leadership

Engagement initiatives measured by retention and satisfaction scores — not activity

Workforce planning and succession with the executive team

Act as trusted advisor to department heads on people challenges



How We Measure Success

P&C Coordinator hired and operating within 60 days

Monthly performance review cycle running across all divisions with department heads facilitating

Roles filled in under 30 days, every time

Friday Self Assessment completion above 90% with weekly trend reports actioned

Zero employment compliance breaches

Employee satisfaction above 4 out of 5 on quarterly surveys



This Role Is For You If

You have led HR in a 65+ person, multi-entity, fast-moving environment and thrived without hand-holding

You have built HR frameworks from scratch and then trained others to run them

You are comfortable influencing department heads who do not report to you

You know how to hire well — and can build a process that finds A-players consistently

You measure your work by business outcomes: retention, review completion, time-to-fill — not activity



This Role Is NOT For You If

You want to be the one running every review, handling every HR ticket, and staying in execution permanently

You have not been a HR leader in a 65+ person organization

You struggle to influence stakeholders who do not report to you

You have never led or managed a direct report

You have only maintained HR systems — never designed one from the ground up

You need to be told what to work on each week



Base Salary: PHP 100,
,000php per month + bonus

Work Style: Fully remote

Hours: Aligned to Australian business hours (AEST/AEDT)

Reports To: Group Operations Leader



We use VideoAsk for initial screening. Do not send a CV
.

You will be asked to complete a short async video screening (6 questions, approximately 15 minutes). Instructions will be sent after you express interest via our careers page or the link provided. 



Applications submitted via OnlineJobs or without a completed VideoAsk will not be reviewed.



To apply, please click on this link:

title: Quantity Surveyor / Estimator
description:
About BCF Contracting Group

BCF Contracting Group Corporation is an award-winning luxury residential renovation and construction company serving the Greater Toronto Area. Since 2010, we've specialized in high-end condo renovations, full home builds, and custom construction projects that demand precision, craftsmanship, and attention to detail. Our portfolio includes some of Toronto's most prestigious residential properties, and our reputation is built on delivering exceptional results on time and on budget.

The Opportunity

We're expanding our operations and seeking an experienced Quantity Surveyor/Estimator to join our team. This is a key role that will relieve management of estimating and quantity take-off responsibilities, allowing us to scale operations and improve project delivery. You will own all aspects of quantity surveying, material takeoffs, cost estimation, and technical review of construction drawings.

Key Responsibilities

• Conduct detailed quantity take-offs and material estimates from architectural and technical drawings

• Prepare comprehensive project estimates including labour, materials, and overhead

• Review construction drawings for accuracy, completeness, and compliance with Ontario Building Code standards

• Analyze project scope and identify potential issues or cost drivers early in the estimation process

• Support multi-discipline projects including structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and finish work

• Maintain accurate records of estimates, revisions, and project documentation

• Collaborate with project managers, site supervisors, and subcontractors to refine estimates and track actuals

• Support bid development and proposal preparation for new projects

Required Qualifications

• Minimum 5 years of professional estimating and quantity surveying experience in North America

• Strong knowledge of Ontario Building Code (OBC) and relevant construction standards

• Multi-discipline expertise across residential construction including structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and finish trades

• Proficiency with digital takeoff and estimating software (e.g., Bluebeam, PlanSwift, or equivalent)

• Ability to read and interpret architectural, structural, mechanical, and electrical drawings

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy in quantifying and costing projects

• Excellent organizational and time management skills, with ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

• Professional communication skills and ability to collaborate effectively with diverse teams

Preferred Qualifications

• Experience in luxury or high-end residential renovation and construction

• Knowledge of condo-specific renovation challenges and requirements

• Certification or formal training in quantity surveying or construction estimating

• Experience with construction management software (Planswift, Square takeoff, Clear Estimates & Buildertrend.)

What We Offer

• Competitive salary commensurate with experience

• Opportunity to work on high-profile luxury residential projects

• Supportive team environment with clear growth potential

• Flexible work arrangements and work-life balance

How to Apply

Interested candidates are invited to submit the following:

• Current resume/CV

• Cover letter describing your relevant experience

• Portfolio or examples of past estimates and takeoff work (if available)

title: ML Engineer / Software Developer
description:
We’re hiring a full-stack LLM engineer to build and scale AI systems for a regulated life-sciences content platform.



This is a hands-on role focused on evaluation systems, retrieval pipelines, observability, and experimentation. You’ll be working across the full stack — from prompt systems and multi-agent orchestration to infrastructure, dashboards, and testing frameworks.



This role is not for surface-level AI users. We’re looking for someone who understands how LLM systems actually work in production — and can build reliable, scalable solutions.



What You’ll Work On



Build and improve LLM evaluation systems (golden sets, regression testing, scoring)

Implement retrieval systems (pgvector, hybrid search, reranking)

Develop multi-agent workflows (chains, retries, structured outputs)

Create observability dashboards (cost, latency, failures)

Run experiments and A/B tests across prompts and models

Ensure accuracy, grounding, and compliance in outputs



Core Requirements



Strong experience with TypeScript / Node.js

Production experience with LLMs (Claude API or similar)

Experience with PostgreSQL / Supabase (RLS)

Familiarity with Edge Functions, async jobs, and deployment (Vercel, Railway)

Strong understanding of prompt engineering, structured outputs, and system design

Experience building or working with evaluation frameworks and testing pipelines



Nice to Have



Experience with tools like Langfuse, Braintrust, Promptfoo

Knowledge of guardrails / safety layers

Familiarity with dashboards (React, Recharts)



This is a long-term role with high ownership. You’ll be building core infrastructure that directly impacts product quality and performance.





Only applicants who applied here would be entertained. Please apply through this link:

title: Senior Customer Support Specialist / Team Lead
description:
About the Role

Charm is hiring a Customer Service Team Lead to support a US-based pool equipment manufacturer specializing in pool pumps and heat pumps. You will be the founding CS hire on this account, which means you're walking into a build-it-as-we-go environment with clear runway to grow a team underneath you as call volume scales.



Your day-to-day centers on inbound phone calls from US customers. Roughly 80% of calls will start as information-gathering: intake the issue, capture the right details in Zendesk, and loop in the client's internal sales, technical, and service teams through Microsoft Teams for live or near-live answers. As the product knowledge base matures over the first 60 to 90 days, you'll transition toward handling more calls end-to-end independently.



This role is ideal for a senior CS professional who wants to anchor a growing account, bring structure to ambiguity, and grow into managing a team.



Key Responsibilities

• Handle inbound customer service calls from US-based customers, primarily covering pool pumps and heat pumps

• Capture and document customer information accurately in Zendesk using existing forms, fields, and macros

• Escalate technical questions through a dedicated Microsoft Teams channel to reach sales reps, technical specialists, and service staff

• Send follow-up responses via
 once answers are gathered, using approved templates and macros

• Flag patterns in customer inquiries to inform knowledge base development

• Maintain high standards for ticket quality, response time, and customer experience

• Collaborate with Charm leadership on SOP refinement and performance reporting

• As the account grows, help interview, onboard, and mentor additional agents



Why This Role

You're coming in as the founding CS hire on this account. We're looking for a self-starter with a strong work ethic who cares about building lasting teams. If you execute, you won't stay solo for long. Charm's goal is to build a team around you over the next 6 to 12 months, and the person who proves out in this seat gets first dibs on leading that team.



If you're interested, please view the requirements and apply here:

title: Experienced Cold Caller / Telemarketer for U.S. Insurance Leads
description:
Job Title: Experienced Cold Caller / Telemarketer for U.S. Insurance Leads

Company: DialXpress

Location: Remote

Job Type: Full-time

Shift: U.S. business hours, Monday to Friday

Experience Required: Minimum 2 years cold calling or telemarketing experience



About Us

DialXpress is a U.S.-based outbound calling company serving insurance agencies nationwide. The company focuses on calling x-dates, internet leads, aged leads, former customers, and requotes, with a live-caller model only. DialXpress positions itself as an approved vendor and emphasizes compliant calling, high dial volume, and warm handoffs for quoting.



About the Role

We are hiring an experienced cold caller / telemarketer who knows how to get through lists, handle objections, speak clearly, and generate real conversations with U.S. prospects. This is not an entry-level role. We want someone who is comfortable making high-volume outbound calls, following call flows, updating the CRM properly, and helping produce quote opportunities for insurance agencies.



You will be calling leads provided by the client or campaign, using approved scripts and processes. DialXpress provides the staffing side of the operation, while clients provide the lead data, dialer access, and campaign instructions.



What You’ll Be Doing



Make outbound calls to U.S. leads daily

Work internet leads, aged leads, x-dates, former customers, and requote opportunities

Speak with prospects professionally and confidently

Follow campaign scripts while still sounding natural

Qualify interest and transfer or hand off warm prospects when appropriate

Log call outcomes, notes, dispositions, and follow-up actions correctly

Follow compliance rules and do-not-call procedures

Maintain strong daily activity and productivity standards

Communicate with management about lead quality, objections, and call results



What We’re Looking For



At least 2 years of experience in cold calling, telemarketing, appointment setting, or outbound sales

Strong spoken English with a clear and neutral accent

Confident on live calls

Good objection handling skills

Comfortable with repetitive outbound work and performance tracking

Reliable internet and quiet work environment

Experience calling U.S. consumers or business owners is preferred

Insurance lead calling experience is a major plus

Experience using dialers, CRMs, and call disposition systems is preferred



Important Requirements



Must be comfortable making a high volume of outbound calls daily (400+)

Must follow scripts, compliance rules, and company processes

Must update records accurately after calls

Must show up consistently and be dependable

Must be coachable and open to feedback



What Makes This Role Different



Live-caller environment only

No robocalls

No AI voice

Warm handoff style campaigns when applicable

DialXpress is built around lead engagement and structured follow-up cadence, including immediate engagement and extended follow-up windows.



Compensation



Hourly rate: $2.00-$3.00

Full-time schedule: 9am-8pm EST (9am-5pm EST Early Shift) (12pm-8pm Late Shift)

Start date: ASAP



How to Apply

To apply, send:



Your resume

A short voice recording introducing yourself

A summary of your cold calling / telemarketing experience

Any experience you have with insurance, live transfers, appointment setting, or U.S. outbound calling

Your internet speed and computer setup



Application Screening Question

Please answer these with your application:



How many years of cold calling experience do you have?

Have you called U.S. leads before?

What industries have you called in?

Are you comfortable with high-volume outbound calling every day?

Have you worked with dialers and CRMs before?

What is your expected hourly rate?



If you want, I can turn this into a more aggressive OnlineJobs.ph-style version optimized to attract stronger sales reps instead of general telemarketers.

title: Experienced Bookkeeper (QuickBooks + Dext Expert)
description:
We are hiring an experienced Bookkeeper on behalf of a Canada-based professional bookkeeping firm that supports growing businesses with clean, accurate, and compliant financials.



This is not an entry-level role.



We are looking for someone who truly understands bookkeeping inside and out—someone who can jump into QuickBooks and Dext confidently, work independently, and maintain high standards with minimal supervision.



If you love clean books, reconciliations, and structured workflows—and you take pride in accuracy—this role is for you.



What You’ll Be Responsible For

- Full-cycle bookkeeping using QuickBooks Online

- Daily and monthly transaction categorization

- Bank and credit card reconciliations

- Managing receipts and documents using Dext

- Reviewing, matching, and validating expenses

- Identifying discrepancies and resolving issues proactively

- Supporting month-end and year-end bookkeeping tasks

- Maintaining organized, audit-ready financial records

- Communicating clearly with the internal team when clarification is needed



What We’re Looking For (Must-Haves)

- Minimum 5 years of hands-on bookkeeping experience

- Expert-level QuickBooks Online knowledge

- Strong experience using Dext 

- Excellent attention to detail and accuracy

- Strong understanding of bookkeeping best practices

- Ability to work independently and manage your own workload

- Reliable internet connection and availability during PST business hours

- Professional written and verbal English communication



Nice-to-Haves (Assets)

- Experience supporting Canadian businesses

- Experience working with accounting firms or bookkeeping agencies

- Familiarity with month-end close processes

- Comfortable working with multiple client files



Why This Role Is a Great Opportunity

- Stable, long-term role with a professional Canadian firm

- Clear processes and expectations

- Opportunity to increase hours based on performance

- Work with a team that values accuracy, accountability, and professionalism

- No micromanagement—your expertise is trusted



How to Apply

- Please include the following in your application:

- A brief summary of your bookkeeping experience

- Your years of experience with QuickBooks Online

- Your experience level with Dext (be specific)

- Examples of bookkeeping tasks you’ve handled independently

- Your availability during PST hours



Only applicants with relevant bookkeeping experience will be considered.

title: E-commerce Growth Manager (Amazon Experience | Focus on Mercado Libre)
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled E-commerce Growth Manager with strong Amazon experience.



While your background in Amazon is important, your primary responsibility will be to manage and lead the growth of our Mercado Libre account.



This role is ideal for someone who understands e-commerce fundamentals—PPC, listings, inventory, and profitability—and is ready to apply those skills to scale a new marketplace.



Responsibilities:



Mercado Libre Account Growth (Primary Focus)

- Take full ownership of our Mercado Libre account

- Develop and execute strategies to drive sales growth

- Optimize listings, pricing, and overall marketplace performance

- Monitor account health and ensure platform compliance



Amazon Account Support (Secondary)

- Support ongoing optimization of Amazon accounts

- Apply best practices from Amazon to improve performance across platforms



Inventory Management

- Monitor inventory levels and turnover

- Coordinate with the Supply Chain team to prevent stockouts and overstocking



Listing Optimization

- Collaborate with the Creative team to improve product listings and content

- Continuously optimize for higher conversion rates



Paid Ads Management

- Manage and optimize ad campaigns (Amazon & Mercado Libre if applicable)

- Monitor key metrics: CTR, CPC, CVR, ACOS, ROAS

- Adjust bids, budgets, and targeting to improve performance



Data & Strategy

- Analyze sales and traffic data regularly

- Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis

- Identify growth opportunities and implement strategies



Profitability & Reporting

- Monitor P&L and ensure profitability targets are met

- Maintain TACOS targets where applicable



Team Collaboration

- Work closely with the Gen VA team for account support



Requirements:

- Proven experience managing Amazon Seller Central accounts

- Strong understanding of Amazon PPC (ACOS, TACOS, ROAS)

- Experience in listing optimization and conversion improvement

- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

- Willingness to learn and lead Mercado Libre account growth

Tools Used:

Helium 10 (H10)

DataDive

Scale Insights

SoStocked



Important Note:

We are a fast-paced company. While we work as a team, each member is expected to take full ownership of their role and work independently. Please apply only if you are confident in your ability to meet these expectations.



To Apply, Please Include:

- Any experience with Mercado Libre (if applicable)

title: Commercial Construction Project Estimator
description:
Join a commercial construction-related equipment manufacturer and play a key role in preparing accurate estimates and project bids to help them win more business.



This is not just a data entry role. You’ll be expected to think through the details, spot gaps, and make sure everything makes sense before pricing a project. You’ll have ownership of your work and contribute to building clear, accurate, and competitive proposals.



We’re looking for someone detail-oriented, reliable, and experienced in construction bidding who can manage multiple projects at the same time.



Responsibilities:

- Review project plans, specifications, drawings, and scope to understand requirements.

- Develop detailed cost estimates, proposals, and bid submissions.

- Interpret and annotate drawings based on project needs and constraints.

- Handle multiple bid requests simultaneously and meet deadlines.

- Work closely with project stakeholders, including Owners, Project Managers, Superintendents, and Subcontractors.

- Reach out to suppliers and subcontractors to gather pricing and ensure accurate proposals.

- Prepare and communicate complete proposals and estimates based on project requirements.

- Assist in pricing discussions and adjustments to stay competitive.



Requirements:

- 3+ years of experience with commercial construction bidding, whether as a supplier, general contractor, or subcontractor.

- 1+ year WFH experience supporting US-based clients.

- Experience with communicating and negotiating pricing is a major plus.

- Strong English communication skills, both written and verbal.

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable juggling multiple tasks.

- Comfortable managing a high volume of bids with accuracy and efficiency.

- Strong communication skills and ability to coordinate with multiple stakeholders.

- Organized, detail-oriented, and able to work independently.

- Must be available to work graveyard hours aligned with U.S. time zones.

- Reliable internet connection and dedicated work-from-home setup required.



Salary and Benefits:

- Base salary: 55,000 PHP per month

- 100% remote / work-from-home

- PTO and paid holidays

 graphics, product visuals, and short-form AI-assisted video content for Meta, TikTok, Instagram, and other digital channels

title: Senior Patient Coordinator & Strategic Logistics VA (PST)
description:
Schedule: Monday – Friday | 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM PST (Pacific Standard Time)



Type: Full-Time (40 Hours/Week)



Rate: $5.00 – $7.00 USD/hour (Based on experience + Night Shift Differential)



We are a specialized foot care business serving a high volume of senior citizens across the West Coast. We are looking for a dedicated, empathetic, and strategically-minded Virtual Assistant to be the "voice" of our company and the manager of our local digital presence.



This is a multi-faceted role. Your primary goal is to ensure our senior patients feel heard and understood, while simultaneously managing the complex logistics of 7 mobile nurses to ensure maximum efficiency and minimum travel costs.



Key Responsibilities



1. Patient Communication & Scheduling (Highest Priority)



The "Senior Voice": Answer incoming calls from elderly patients. You must speak slowly, clearly, and with extreme patience, as many of our clients are hard of hearing.



Empathetic Booking: Help seniors navigate their foot care needs and book appointments with our nursing team using our proprietary scheduling app.



Lead Management: Proactively follow up on missed calls and voi
s to ensure every patient is cared for.



2. Strategic Logistics & Route Optimization



Travel Fee Management: Our app automatically calculates travel expenses. Your job is to strategically schedule patients in "clusters" to trigger the lowest possible travel fees for the clients.



Nurse Operations: Assist our 7 mobile nurses in setting their daily "Base of Operations" within the app to optimize their routes and increase their booking capacity.



Proactive Filling: Monitor nurse schedules for gaps. You will reach out to past patients in specific geographic areas to fill empty slots and maximize nurse productivity.



3. Local SEO & Google Business Profile (GBP) Management



Multi-City Management: Set up and optimize Google Business Profile listings for the various cities we serve.



Local SEO Maintenance: Ensure "NAP" (Name, Address, Phone) consistency across all listings.



Review Management: Monitor and respond to patient reviews with a warm, professional tone.



Google Posts: Create weekly updates on GBP to highlight local availability, travel fee discounts, and foot care tips.



4. Niche Social Media Management



Content Execution: Turn provided foot care tips, app improvements, and other content into professional posts for
 and Instagram (3-4 times per week) using Canva.



Engagement: Monitor and reply to community comments and direct messages.



5. Technical QA (As Needed)



App Testing: Act as our internal Quality Assurance tester. As you use the app for scheduling, document any bugs or user-experience issues with screenshots or recordings (Loom).



Requirements



Perfect Verbal Clarity: A neutral, very clear English accent is mandatory. You must be able to adjust your speaking pace for hard-of-hearing seniors.



Logistics Mindset: You enjoy "Tetris-style" scheduling and finding the most efficient way to organize a map.



Local SEO Knowledge: Experience managing Google Business Profiles (claiming, verifying, and optimizing) is a strong plus.



Tech Proficiency: Proficient in Canva, Meta Business Suite, and Google Workspace.



Medical/Healthcare Background: Previous experience in US healthcare admin or medical billing is highly preferred.



Reliability: High-speed internet with a backup power/data plan. Comfortable working the graveyard shift (PST hours).



How to Apply (MANDATORY)



To be considered, you must submit a resume and a 30-second voice recording (Vocaroo or similar) reading the following script:



"Hello! Thank you for calling our foot care clinic. My name is [Your Name]. I see that Nurse Jenny is going to be in your neighborhood this Wednesday. If we schedule you for 2:00 PM, I can actually reduce your travel fee because she is already in the area. Would that work for you?"



Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed.

title: Web Designer
description:
Job Posting: Web Designer at ProViaXX



ProViaXX is currently seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Web Designer to join our growing team. As we continue to expand our digital footprint, we require a professional who possesses the technical acumen to build sophisticated, high-performance websites from the ground up. Unlike designers who rely solely on drag-and-drop page builders, our ideal candidate understands the underlying architecture of the web and is proficient in manual coding.



Role Requirements and Responsibilities

The successful candidate will be responsible for the full lifecycle of web development, from initial wireframing and visual design to the implementation of clean, semantic code. You must demonstrate a mastery of core web technologies, specifically HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, as these are the foundational pillars of modern web architecture. A deep understanding of responsive design principles is essential to ensure that our digital assets function seamlessly across all devices and screen sizes. Furthermore, you should be comfortable working with server-side scripting and database management, as our projects often require dynamic content integration.



Application Instructions

If you are a self-motivated developer who thrives on solving complex technical challenges and creating intuitive user experiences, we want to hear from you. To apply for this position, please submit your response to this listing. You must include your active WhatsApp number in your reply. Our hiring team will review your qualifications and reach out via WhatsApp to schedule an initial over-the-phone interview. We look forward to seeing your portfolio and discussing how your coding expertise can contribute to the future of ProViaXX.

title: Video Editor | Connect Social
description:
Video Editor | Direct Response & Social Ads



About Connect Social

Connect Social is a performance advertising agency running campaigns across Meta, Google, TikTok, CTV, Spotify, Rumble, and other platforms for a roster of high-profile media brands and direct response advertisers. We move fast, test aggressively, and measure everything by what drives results - not what looks pretty in a reel.



The Role

We're hiring a full-time Video Editor to own the creative production pipeline for our ad campaigns. You'll cut direct response ads, UGC-style content, podcast clips, and social-native video across every major platform. This is a high-volume, high-iteration role. You'll ship dozens of ad variants per week and see the performance data on every one.



This is a full-time role that requires your full attention during working hours. If you're looking to juggle this alongside other freelance work or another full-time job, this isn't the right fit.



What You'll Do

- Edit direct response video ads for Meta, TikTok, YouTube, etc, from raw footage to platform-ready cuts

- Produce rapid variations of winning ads (hook tests, length variations, aspect ratio adaptations) so our media buyers can keep scaling what works

- Cut podcast and long-form content into scroll-stopping short-form clips

- Build UGC-style ads from creator footage, including pacing, captions, b-roll, sound design, and motion graphics

- Collaborate with our Creative Strategist on concepts, scripts, and testing frameworks

- Use performance data to inform future edits - we'll tell you what's working, you figure out how to make more of it

- Maintain an organized project and asset library so the team can move fast without losing work



What We Expect | The Bar

These aren't aspirations. They're the baseline:

- First drafts ship clean. Zero spelling errors, correct captions, correct logos, correct brand assets, clean audio, clean transitions. These are QC items, not creative calls. You proof your own work before anything leaves your desk.

- Revisions are applied in full, on the first pass. When you get a note list, every bullet gets addressed on every video in the batch. If a note is unclear, you ask before exporting - not after.

- Same-day turnaround on short-form. Edits and revisions on videos under 60 seconds turn around the same day. If something legitimately needs more time, you flag it up front so we can plan - we don't find out when it's late.

- Nothing slips. Every request gets tracked and delivered. "It got lost" is not a phrase we want to hear.

- You catch your own mistakes. We shouldn't have to flag the same error twice. If we do, something is broken in your process and we'll expect you to fix it.



What We're Looking For

- 3+ years editing video professionally, with meaningful experience in direct response or social ads (not just brand content or YouTube creator work)

- Fluency in Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve, plus After Effects for motion graphics

- A reel demonstrating short-form social ads you can point to and say "this performed." Bonus points if you can share actual metrics

- Strong instincts for hooks, pacing, and retention. You understand why the first 3 seconds matter more than the next 30

- Comfortable with high volume and fast turnaround. You'd rather ship 10 good cuts than polish 1 perfect one

- Obsessive attention to detail on the small stuff - captions, spelling, logo placement, export settings. This is non-negotiable

- Solid captioning, color, and sound design skills

- AI-tool literacy (ElevenLabs, Descript, Runway, Topaz, CapCut, Kapwing, etc.). We lean into these and you should too

- Strong written English for captions and communication with the team



Nice to Have

- Podcast clipping experience (Opus Clip, Riverside, Descript workflows)

- Motion graphics and thumbnail design chops

- Comfort on-camera or directing creators for UGC shoots



This Isn't the Right Fit If...

- You need multiple revision rounds to catch basic errors

- You see same-day turnaround on short-form as aggressive rather than normal

- You're planning to split your hours with another job or heavy freelance load. Unacceptable.

- You prefer polishing one hero piece over shipping a dozen tested variants

- You're looking for a brand-content role where aesthetics matter more than performance



Compensation

Competitive base salary commensurate with experience. We'll talk numbers once we know you're the right fit.



How to Apply

Send the following to 

1. Your reel (link)

2. Your resume

3. A short note (under 200 words) on one ad you edited that performed well - what was the hook, why did it work, and what would you test next

4. Your typical turnaround time on a 30-second ad from brief to first draft, and your process for QC before sending

5. Put "Video Editor — [Your First Name]" in the subject line



Applications missing any of the above won't be reviewed. We read carefully, and we expect the same from you.

title: Marketing Account Manager
description:
We are a US-based marketing consulting company that runs done-for-you
 and Instagram ad campaigns for home-service businesses (painters, foundation repair, concrete coatings, truck repair, remodelers, and more). We are growing fast and need a sharp, client-facing Marketing Account Manager to join our team.



You will work directly under our Marketing Team Lead, learning our systems and processes, with the goal of becoming a full Account Manager handling your own portfolio of clients within 30-60 days.



What you will be doing:

- Managing client communication over Slack. Sending updates, following up on deliverables, answering questions, and making sure every client feels taken care of. Your written communication needs to sound natural, professional, and human. Not robotic, not AI-generated.

- Joining video calls with clients for onboarding, check-ins, and performance reviews. You need to be comfortable on camera with a professional setup and strong English.

- Creating static ad images in Canva. You will design scroll-stopping
 and Instagram ad graphics based on creative briefs from our team. This includes single image ads, carousels, before-and-after graphics, and offer graphics.

- Writing
 ad scripts for video ads (45 seconds long). These scripts need to sound like a real person talking, not like ChatGPT wrote them. You will learn our proven ad framework and write scripts tailored to each client's business.

Following up with clients to collect assets we need. Photos, videos, raw footage, logo files, Meta access, CRM setup. You are the person making sure nothing stalls.

- Building and uploading ad campaigns in Meta Ads Manager based on direction from our team lead.

- Learning GoHighLevel (our CRM) and eventually managing client pipelines, automations, and lead flow.

Reporting on ad performance. Pulling numbers, putting together simple weekly updates, and flagging anything that needs attention.



What we need from you:

- Excellent written and spoken English. This is non-negotiable. You will be writing client-facing messages daily and joining video calls weekly. Your English needs to be fluent and natural.

- Strong Canva skills. You should be able to take a creative brief and produce polished, professional ad graphics without needing constant direction. Send us examples of your work.

- Understanding of Meta Business Suite and
 Ads Manager. You don't need to be an expert but you need to know your way around the platform. If you've run ads before or managed ad accounts, that's a big plus.

- Ability to write ad copy and video scripts that sound like a real person talking. We will train you on our framework but you need to have a natural writing voice. If everything you write sounds like it came from AI, this is not the right fit.

- Good camera and internet for video calls. You will be on client calls regularly. Blurry webcam and choppy audio is not acceptable. Professional setup required.

- Self-starter who can follow SOPs and execute without being micromanaged. We will train you on our systems but once you learn them, we expect you to run with it.

- GoHighLevel experience is a plus but not required. We will train you.



Details:

Full time, 40 hours per week.

Must be available during US business hours (Central Time, GMT-6).

$300/week paid weekly.

Long-term position. We are growing and there is room to grow with us.

Start date: immediately.



To apply:

Send us the following:

A short video (60 seconds max) introducing yourself in English. Tell us about your experience and why you'd be a good fit. This is how we evaluate your English, your camera quality, and your presence on video.

3-5 examples of Canva ad graphics or marketing designs you have created.

A short writing sample. Take this prompt and write a 30-second
 video ad script for a painting company in Dallas, Texas that charges $2,500 for a full exterior paint job and offers a 5-year warranty. We want to see your natural writing voice.

Your Meta Ads Manager experience level (none, beginner, intermediate, advanced).

How soon you can start.

title: Part-Time Social Media Manager — Faux & Dried Flower Rental Business
description:
We are looking for a creative, self-motivated social media manager to run the Instagram account for a growing faux and dried flower rental business. This is a part-time, ongoing role.



ABOUT THE BUSINESS:

This business rents beautiful faux and dried flower arrangements for events, photo shoots, and home styling. We have existing footage and photos that can be used for content creation.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING:

- Create and post 5 times per week on Instagram

- Produce a mix of static posts and Reels using provided footage and your own creative direction

- Write captions, select hashtags, and maintain a consistent brand aesthetic

- Manage and grow the Instagram account over time

- Engage with followers and respond to comments/DMs as needed



REQUIREMENTS:

- Proven experience managing Instagram for a product or lifestyle brand (please include account handles or links)

- Comfortable editing short-form video (Reels)

- Eye for visual aesthetics, particularly in the home, events, or wedding niche

- Consistent availability and reliable communication



PAY:

We are looking for the most competitive rate for the scope of work described. Please include your monthly rate in your application. Candidates who provide a clear breakdown of what is included in their rate will be prioritized.



TO APPLY:

We get a lot of generic applications and want to find someone who has genuinely read this listing. Please begin your application with the phrase "I understand the flower niche" so we know you read through everything. Then share 2–3 Instagram accounts you have managed and tell us one content idea you would create for a faux flower rental business. Applications without this phrase will not be reviewed.



We look forward to seeing your creativity!

title: Marketing & Growth Strategist
description:
About Us



We are building structured, system-driven operations to support long-term growth. Our marketing is education-focused, data-informed, and execution-driven.



About the Role

We are hiring a Marketing & Growth Coordinator to support target market research, content creation, analytics tracking, and
 marketing. 



This is a structured execution role, not a strategy role.

This role creates and prepares marketing assets (blogs, short-form content, images, basic video edits) and supports publishing and campaign execution within established workflows.



Responsibilities

• Research and document target markets, customer segments, and audience needs

• Develop content ideas aligned with brand positioning and marketing goals

• Write SEO-aligned blog content and marketing copy

• Create short-form content (social posts, reels, short videos) with basic editing

• Design on-brand graphics and visuals using Canva or similar tools

• Schedule and publish social media content according to plan

• Create, format, and send
 marketing campaigns

• Review analytics (Google Analytics and platform insights) to evaluate performance

• Organize content assets, drafts, and documentation within shared systems

• Follow established workflows, brand guidelines, and deadlines consistently



Tools & Systems You Will Use

Some prior experience is required. This is not a zero-experience role. You should already be comfortable working inside these tools.

• Google Analytics (basic reporting and performance review)

• Short-form video tools — Canva, CapCut, or similar (basic editing only)

•

You Must Already Be Comfortable

• Working from content briefs and instructions

• Interpreting basic analytics data

• Writing clearly and professionally

• Managing tasks and deadlines inside a system



Experience Requirements

• Minimum 4 years of professional marketing or content-related experience

• Experience working with analytics, written content, and structured workflows

This role is not suitable for students or entry-level applicants.

 marketing campaigns

, or
