title: FOUNDER'S RIGHT-HAND EA / OPERATIONS MANAGER
description:
FOUNDER'S RIGHT-HAND EA / OPERATIONS MANAGER

Remote · Full-Time · Growth Path to Chief of Staff

40 hrs/week · Min. 4 hrs overlap with US Pacific Time



This is not a task-runner role. This is a values-driven, growth-oriented position for someone who wants to be part of building something meaningful — and grow into a long-term leadership seat.



WHO WE ARE



We are a marketing, media, and personal brand company built around one founder's vision. At the center of everything is a message about ownership, discipline, personal growth, and becoming the best version of yourself so you can show up fully for the people around you.



We believe that structure and routines are what set people free — not hold them back. We build with intention, hold ourselves to a high standard, and show up every day committed to getting better.



If that resonates with you at a personal level — not just professionally — keep reading.



THE ROLE



You are the founder's right hand — remote, reliable, and deeply aligned with his vision.



Your job is to learn how Anthony thinks, what he values, and how he makes decisions — so you can start operating on his behalf, taking things off his plate, and getting things to a final draft for his approval instead of bringing him raw problems.



This role is EA-first. You will handle calendar management, protect his time, manage follow-ups, and be the go-between for Anthony and the people around him. But you are also a systems thinker — you take Anthony's ideas and vision, turn them into a manageable project, and then check in daily to make sure the team is aligned and moving. If there are roadblocks, you problem-solve first. If it's beyond your scope, you bring it to Anthony — clearly and with options.



Your most important job is keeping Anthony in the zone — executing on the things only he can do.



WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO



• Manage Anthony's calendar — block his time, protect his deep work, and make sure he is never double-booked

• Be the go-between for Anthony and the team, clients, and guests — so he is not the bottleneck

• Take his vision and ideas and turn them into structured, manageable projects with clear next steps

• Once priorities are set with Anthony, personally drive progress on those goals — tracking, following up, and keeping the team accountable so things actually move

• Check in daily with Anthony and the team to make sure things are on track and aligned

• Identify roadblocks early — problem-solve what you can, escalate what you can't with options in hand

• Help ideate and generate content directions for the podcast and personal brand

• Reach out to potential podcast guests, manage scheduling, and make sure guests show up prepared and on time

• Handle follow-ups so nothing falls through the cracks

• Actively use and explore AI tools to save time, cut costs, and help Anthony's message reach more people faster

• Protect Anthony's time so he can focus on what only he can do — creating, thinking, and leading



WHO YOU ARE



You are someone who:



• Has worked directly with an entrepreneur or founder before and thrived in that environment

• Understands that entrepreneurial energy means a lot of ideas — and your job is to bring them back down to the main priorities and execute

• Has managed other people, reviewed their work, held them to a standard, and given real feedback

• Is genuinely curious about AI and how to use it to save time, systematize processes, and get a message to more people faster — candidates actively exploring AI tools will be prioritized

• Is deeply personally growth-oriented — you read, you reflect, you work on yourself

• Believes in discipline, ownership, and routines — not as rigid rules but as the foundation for freedom

• Is a positive, stable, grounding presence — the kind of person others feel calm around

• Either wants to build your own business someday or is passionate about supporting someone who is building one

• Is extremely organized and highly detail-oriented — you catch what others miss and nothing falls through the cracks

• Has experience with Asana, Notion, or similar project management tools

• Can capture thoughts, ideas, and brain-dumps quickly and accurately — turning them into something actionable or something to revisit later

• Communicates in clear, clean English — written and verbal



You exercise regularly. You eat to support your body. You invest in your relationships, your faith, and your mental and emotional health. The people around you might think you are a little intense about wanting better for yourself and your family — and you would not have it any other way.



You believe God has a plan for your life and you show up every day to honor it. Faith and discipline are not opposites to you. They work together.



You know there is more opportunity out there than what is immediately around you and you are willing to work hard to reach it. That hunger is not a burden — it is what drives you.



We have high standards here and we do not apologize for them. We are not looking to drag anyone along or ask anyone to be something they are not. We are looking for someone already on their own path — because when people with high standards are in the same room, everyone gets better.



The skill sets matter — but what we are really looking for is a growth mindset so strong that the skills become almost secondary. If you are obsessed with learning and getting better, you will figure out what you need to figure out. That drive is what we cannot teach — and it is what will determine how far you go here.



You will be challenged here. You will be encouraged here. And if you are the right person, you will be elevated here.



If your instinct is to wait to be told what to do, this won't be the right fit.

If your instinct is to see what needs to happen and make it happen — welcome home.



THE GROWTH PATH



We are transparent about where this role goes for the right person:



• Months 1–3: Learn Anthony's world — his voice, his vision, his decision-making. Handle EA tasks, protect his calendar, and start building systems.

• Months 3–6: Take on more ownership. Start representing Anthony in communications and managing the team with more independence.

• 6–12 months: Functioning as a remote Operations Lead — running the day-to-day with minimal oversight.

• Year 2+: Chief of Staff track — for the right person, this seat grows into something significant.



As we grow and develop together, we will hone this in. But that is the structure and the intention. We invest in people who invest in the mission. Compensation grows as the role and trust grow.



One of the key factors in your growth path and compensation will be this: the more you can delegate and elevate — to a tea
mber or to AI — the more bandwidth you free up to work with Anthony on bigger ideas. We are not rewarding busyness. We are rewarding leverage. The person who figures out how to get more done through others and through smart systems, and then uses that freed-up space to think and build at a higher level, is the person who grows fastest here.



ROLE DETAILS



• Full-time, 40 hours per week

• Remote — work from anywhere

• Must have at least 4 hours of overlap with US Pacific Standard Time daily

• Starting compensation: $800–$1,200/month based on experience, with real growth tied to performance

• Strong English communication required — written and verbal

• Reliable internet and a dedicated, distraction-free workspace required



HOW TO APPLY — READ THIS CAREFULLY



We have designed this process intentionally. If you cannot follow these steps exactly, this is not the role for you. If you can — it tells us everything we need to know about how you operate.



STEP 1: RECORD A 90-SECOND VIDEO



Record a 90-second video answering the three questions below. Use Loom, your phone, or any recording tool. Keep it natural — we want to hear who you actually are, not a rehearsed performance.



Q1. How are you currently using AI — and how do you think about it as a tool?

We are not looking for someone who uses ChatGPT to write
s. We want to know if you are genuinely curious about what AI can do — how it can save time, automate repetitive work, and free up brain space for bigger thinking. Tell us what you are actually doing with it, what you are learning, and how you see it fitting into a role like this. If you are not using AI yet, tell us honestly why and what you are curious about.



Q2. What excites you about this role — and how do you think you can add value?

We want to know if you have worked in an entrepreneurial environment before, what drew you to this opportunity specifically, and how your background sets you up to contribute. Be specific — we can tell the difference between someone who read the job post and someone who actually connected with it.



Q3. Where do you want to go? What are your future goals — personally or professionally?

We are looking for someone growth-oriented who has a vision for their own life. Tell us about yours.



STEP 2:
 US THE NEXT DAY AT 9:00 AM PST



Send your video link and a brief introduction to 



Applications sent at the wrong time or without the correct subject line will not be reviewed. This is not a test of your tech skills — it is a test of your attention to detail and your ability to follow through.



YOUR
 SUBJECT LINE MUST READ EXACTLY:

"Founder's Right Hand — [Your Full Name]"

Applications without this exact subject line will not be opened.



STEP 3: WE REVIEW AND RESPOND



We review every video personally. Candidates who move forward will be contacted for a brief interview. We move quickly when we find the right person.



We are not just filling a seat.

We are looking for someone who believes in what we are building and wants to grow alongside it.

title: Agency Systems & Operations Manager
description:
Who We Are



Legacy House Marketing is a husband-and-wife-owned digital marketing agency based in Georgetown, TX. We work with local service businesses, contractors, home services, trades, etc., and help them build a real presence online that actually drives results.

We have full-time editors, a creative team of videographers and photographers, and a growing client base. We're running campaigns, delivering results, and expanding. Now we're making intentional investments in the systems and infrastructure that will take us to the next level, and we need a sharp operator to help lead that effort.

That's why we're hiring.



The Role



This is a builder role first, operator role second.

We're not looking for someone to manage a system we've already built. We're looking for someone who can look at what we have, identify what's missing, and build the infrastructure that lets this agency scale without everything running through the owners.

You'll start by getting into the weeds: auditing our ClickUp setup, our GHL workflows, our automations, and our internal processes. You'll identify the gaps. Then you'll build the structure, document the SOPs, and create the systems that make our team faster, our clients happier, and our operation something we can actually hand off and grow.

Once the foundation is built, you'll run it. You'll be the operational backbone of Legacy House Marketing.



What You'll Own:



Phase 1 — Build (First 30-60 Days)

 ClickUp Architecture

 - Audit our current ClickUp setup and identify what's working and what isn't

 - Build out a clean, scalable workspace structure; spaces, folders, lists, dashboards, and templates for every core function of the agency

 - Create task templates for recurring workflows, including client onboarding, content calendar management, reporting, and ad campaign setup

 - Build dashboards that give ownership a real-time view of client status, deliverables, and team workload

 - Document every process as an SOP so anyone on the team can follow it without asking questions 



GHL Systems

 - Audit existing GHL workflows, pipelines, and automations

 - Identify gaps and build out what's missing; lead nurture sequences, onboarding workflows, client communication automations, and internal notifications

 - Build and maintain client landing pages inside GHL, including form setup, automation connections, and pipeline integration

 - Connect client Meta ad accounts,
 pages, and Instagram profiles to GHL as part of the standard onboarding process

 - Complete A2P registration for clients inside GHL — managing the submission process, tracking approval status, and ensuring compliance requirements are met

 - Organize sub-accounts, pipelines, and tags so everything is clean and findable

 - Build and document SOPs for every GHL process the team will use repeatedly



Integrations

 - Connect GHL with third-party tools using Make or Zapier where needed

 - Build and maintain integrations between GHL, ClickUp, Google Drive, Meta Business Suite, and any other tools in our stack

 - Ensure data flows cleanly between platforms so nothing has to be entered twice

 - Troubleshoot integration failures and broken automations quickly and independently

 - Document every integration so it can be maintained or updated without you being the only one who understands it



Automations & Workflows

 - Build multi-step automations that reduce manual work across the agency

 - Create internal notification workflows so the right people are alerted at the right time

 - Build client-facing automations, including onboarding sequences, follow-up workflows, and reporting triggers

 - Troubleshoot existing automations that aren't working correctly

 - Continuously look for opportunities to eliminate manual tasks through smart automation



Internal Process Documentation

 - Turn what currently lives in our heads into written, repeatable SOPs

 - Create onboarding checklists, content workflow docs, reporting templates, and client communication frameworks

 - Build a central operations hub in ClickUp where the whole team knows where to find what they need



Phase 2 — Run (Ongoing)

Once the systems are built, you own the day-to-day operation:



Client Onboarding

 - Execute the full client onboarding checklist from signed contract through first deliverable

 - Set up client folders, store brand assets, and organize task boards in ClickUp

 - Send and organize intake forms and onboarding documents

 - Connect client Meta accounts to GHL and complete A2P registration for every new client

 - Build client landing pages in GHL and connect all forms to the appropriate workflows and pipelines

 - Assist clients with Meta Business Suite access and calendar setup as needed



Project Coordination

 - Maintain the master content calendar across all clients in ClickUp

 - Track deliverables, deadlines, and project progress

 - Coordinate between the creative team and clients to keep projects moving

 - Flag anything falling behind before it becomes a problem



Social Media Operations

 - Organize client social media logins and access

 - Upload and schedule posts using Meta Business Suite or our scheduling tool

 - Add cover photos, tag locations, format captions, and ensure posts go out on time and meet platform specs



Reporting & Analytics

 - Compile and deliver monthly performance reports for each client

 - Track weekly ad analytics and create internal reports for each client 

 - Maintain reporting templates and ensure consistency across all client reports



Systems Maintenance

 - Keep tech stack (ClickUp, GHL, Slack, and Google Drive) organized and up to date

 - Maintain and improve SOPs as the agency grows and processes evolve

 - Proactively identify inefficiencies and fix them before they become problems



What We're Looking For:



Non-negotiables:

 - ClickUp power user; you've built workspaces from scratch, not just worked inside someone else's setup

 - GHL intermediate to advanced; workflows, automations, pipelines, landing page builder, sub-account management, and A2P registration

 - Hands-on experience with Meta Business Suite integrations; you've connected client ad accounts, pages, and Instagram profiles to GHL before

 - A2P experience: you understand the registration process in GHL, the requirements, and how to get it done correctly

 - Make or Zapier proficiency; you've built real multi-step cross-platform automations, not just simple triggers

 - Systems thinker; when you look at a messy operation, your instinct is to organize and document it, not work around it

 - Clear communicator and SOP writer; you can turn a complex process into something a new tea
mber can follow without asking questions

 - Self-directed; you don't need someone to tell you what to do next once you understand the goal. We want someone who can take this part of our business and own it.

 - Proactive communicator; you flag issues, ask the right questions, and keep people informed without being chased

 - Stable internet with backup options

 - Overlap with Central Standard Time (CST) required for a minimum of 4 hours per day (MON-FRI)



Strong advantages:

 - Experience working inside a marketing agency

 - Familiarity with Google Drive organization and folder architecture

 - Comfortable with basic client-facing communication when needed

 - Experience building landing pages inside GHL specifically



What This Is Not:

 - This is not a VA role where you wait for tasks to be assigned

 - This is not a social media manager or content creator role

 - This is not a role for someone who needs a fully built system handed to them before they can work

 - This is not a passive position — we need a builder, not a passenger



Why This Role Matters:

When the systems are built and running, the Legacy House Marketing founders can focus entirely on sales, growth, and creative direction. The agency can take on more clients without breaking. Every future tea
mber we bring on will onboard faster and perform better because of the infrastructure this person builds.

This isn't a dead-end role. It's a ground-floor opportunity inside a fast-growing agency. The person who builds this foundation will grow with it; more responsibility, more ownership, and more compensation as we scale. We take care of the people who help us win.



Compensation & Details

Type: Part-time to start, with a clear path to full-time based on performance and agency growth

Location: Remote

Pay: $500 – $700/month to start, with a structured compensation review at 90 days based on performance

Hours: Approximately 20–25 hours per week

Timezone: Overlap with Central Standard Time (CST) required for a minimum of 4 hours per day



How to Apply:

Send an
 to 
 with the subject line: "I'm Your Systems Person"

Include:

A brief description of a system or operational structure you built from scratch — what it was, what tools you used, and what changed after

Your honest experience level with ClickUp and GHL specifically — be specific, not general

Your experience with Meta integrations and A2P registration — have you done this before, and how many times

An automation you've built in Make or Zapier that you're proud of

We read every application. We move quickly. If you're the right fit, we will let you know within a few days.



A Note From Us:

We're building an agency that we're genuinely proud of; one that serves clients well, runs smoothly, and supports the life we're building for our family. We're not looking for someone to fill a seat. We're looking for someone who sees a growing operation and gets excited about building the infrastructure behind it.

If that's you, we want to hear from you.

— Jonathan & Jana-Marie, Legacy House Marketing

title: Book Keeper
description:
******MUST SUBMIT A RESUME AND VOICE RECORDING TO BE CONSIDERED FOR THE ROLE************





Position Overview:



We are looking for a dependable and detail-oriented Bookkeeper to join our team and take ownership of our day-to-day financial operations. This role is essential to maintaining the accuracy, organization, and integrity of our financial records. The ideal candidate is someone who enjoys working with numbers, has a strong sense of accountability, and can manage multiple financial tasks with precision and consistency.



As a Bookkeeper, you will play a key role in supporting the financial health of the organization by ensuring that all transactions are recorded properly and that accounts are kept up to date. You will work closely with management to provide clear visibility into financial performance and help ensure that all financial processes run smoothly.



Key Responsibilities:



In this role, you will be responsible for maintaining and updating financial records, including tracking daily transactions such as income, expenses, and payments. You will handle accounts payable and receivable, ensuring invoices are issued accurately and payments are collected and recorded in a timely manner. Regular reconciliation of bank and credit card statements will be part of your routine to ensure all records align correctly.



You will also assist with payroll processing, maintain organized financial documentation, and prepare periodic financial reports that provide insight into the company’s financial position. Attention to detail is critical, as you will be expected to identify discrepancies and resolve them efficiently. Additionally, you will help ensure compliance with applicable financial regulations and internal procedures.



Qualifications and Experience:



The successful candidate will have prior experience in bookkeeping or a similar accounting role, along with a solid understanding of basic accounting principles. Familiarity with accounting software such as QuickBooks, Xero, or similar platforms is important, as well as comfort working with spreadsheets and digital financial tools.



Strong organizational skills and the ability to prioritize tasks are essential in this role, as you will be handling multiple responsibilities at once. You should also demonstrate a high level of discretion and professionalism when working with sensitive financial information.



While not required, a degree in accounting, finance, or a related field, as well as any bookkeeping certifications, would be considered an asset.



Work Environment and Compensation:



This position offers the opportunity to work in a collaborative and supportive environment where accuracy and reliability are highly valued. Compensation will be based on experience and qualifications, and additional benefits may be available depending on employment type.



How to Apply:



If you are a motivated and detail-focused professional looking to contribute to a growing organization, we encourage you to apply. Please submit your resume along with a brief cover letter describing your relevant experience and interest in the role to [Insert
 or application link].

title: Project Managers (5x open roles)
description:
Here's the updated version with the new intro framing — 5 distinct PM roles, each owning a specific team:



---



**Project Manager — 5 Open Roles (Philippines, Remote)**



We're hiring 5 Project Managers to run point on execution across our multi-brand operation. Each role owns a specific team and a specific outcome. These are leadership roles — you'll direct people, track deliverables, hold teammates accountable, and drive work across the finish line on schedule.



If you're someone who naturally organizes chaos, runs tight project boards, and gets energy from shipping work on time — keep reading.



**The 5 Roles**



*Role 1 — Creative Team PM*

Run our creative output engine. Manage the creative database, review work, give feedback, track weekly deliverables, and report on results. Own the execution calendar for ads, graphics, and static creative. You make sure creative ships on time and hits the mark.



*Role 2 — App Building Team PM*

Manage app-building projects across the portfolio. Give feedback, track builds, and ship product. Hands-on capability expected — you should be comfortable building in Lovable, directing AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT), and actively moving projects forward yourself, not just coordinating.



*Role 3 — Social Content Team PM*

Run our social creation team. Manage the content database, review output, give feedback, track weekly deliverables, and track performance. Own execution across AI content, b-roll, and long-form YouTube. You keep the content machine producing on schedule.



*Role 4 — Notion PMT Manager*

Own our Notion project management tooling. Keep it dialed in, clean, and current. Make sure automations and reporting systems are running correctly, and continuously find ways to improve workflows so the team stays organized and focused. You're the connective tissue that keeps everything visible.



*Role 5 — Launch PM (Carnivore Bar Everyday Bar Presale)*

Drive the end-to-end launch of our Everyday Bar presale and funnels. Coordinate across marketing, creative, and ops. Request work from multiple teams, hold them accountable to deadlines, and keep everything tracked in a single Notion project area. Daily accountability on execution. You make this launch a success.



**What All 5 Roles Have In Common**



- You run a team or a project, not a task list

- You track deliverables in Notion and hold people to deadlines

- You communicate clearly, request work cleanly, and follow up without friction

- You own outcomes — not just activity



**Growth Path**



Strong performers grow into:

- Head of Remote Ops

- Operations Director

- Brand Operator (running a specific brand end-to-end)

- Chief of Staff



We promote from within based on performance and ownership.



**Who We're Looking For**



- 2+ years managing projects, teams, or remote operations

- Strong written English and clear, concise communication

- Naturally organized — you think in timelines, owners, and next steps

- Comfortable holding people accountable without creating tension

- Able to work across time zones with a US-based founder and team

- Self-directed — you move work forward without being told what to do next



*Preferred (varies by role)*

- Notion proficiency (databases, views, automations)

- Shopify, Amazon, or DTC e-commerce background

- Familiarity with AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Lovable, Zapier, Make)

- Creative ops or content production experience



**Who This Is For**



Someone who wants to run a team, not just complete tasks. Someone who takes pride in things getting done on time and done well. Someone who wants to grow into a senior operations role inside a fast-moving, AI-native company.



If that's you, tell us which of the 5 roles fits you best and apply.

title: SEO & Affiliate Operations Manager — Gold IRA Niche (Attorney-Led US Site)
description:
SEO & Affiliate Operations Manager — Gold IRA Niche (Attorney-Led US Site)



Salary: $650/month starting, with clear path to $1,200+/month based on performance

Performance Bonus: Up to $200/month additional based on KPIs (traffic, rankings, conversions)

Hours: 8 hours/day, Monday to Friday

Time Zone: Flexible, but 2–3 hours overlap with Israel time (GMT+3) required

Long-term: This is a long-term role. We are building a 7-figure business and want someone who grows with us.





ABOUT US



We are Elite Asset Shield (
), a US-focused affiliate content site in the Gold IRA and Crypto IRA niche. The site is led by a licensed attorney specializing in international asset protection. Our target audience is US investors aged 50+ with significant retirement assets.



We are entering an aggressive growth phase with a clear 12–18 month roadmap to $1M ARR through organic SEO,
, and YouTube. We have the strategy, budget, and systems. We need an execution partner.





WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR



You are NOT a general VA. You are a specialist who has done this before. If you have not personally managed the growth of an affiliate or content site, please do not apply.



You have:



- 2+ years of hands-on SEO experience, ideally in finance, legal, or other YMYL niches

- Proven portfolio of websites you have helped grow (we will ask for URLs and specific metrics)

- Strong English writing and editing skills (American English)

- Working knowledge of: WordPress, Rank Math or Yoast, Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs or SEMrush, Surfer SEO

- Experience with
 marketing platforms (Beehiiv, ConvertKit, or similar)

- Experience coordinating with freelance writers (hiring, briefing, editing, paying)

- Experience with backlink outreach: HARO/Connectively, guest posts, digital PR

- Excellent organizational skills — you can run ClickUp or Notion boards and produce weekly reports without being asked twice



Bonus points for:



- Direct experience in Gold IRA, precious metals, or retirement finance niches

- YouTube channel operations experience

- Basic HTML/CSS for WordPress tweaks

- Canva or basic Photoshop for featured images





WHAT YOU WILL DO



Weekly responsibilities:



- Manage the editorial pipeline: brief writers, review drafts, run SEO optimization in Surfer, publish to WordPress

- Coordinate 10–15 HARO/Connectively submissions per week (attorney writes responses, you handle dispatch and tracking)

- Send 10+ guest post pitches per week to DR 40+ finance sites

- Manage weekly newsletter: draft in our voice, get attorney approval, schedule, track performance

- Monitor Google Search Console for ranking changes, opportunities, and issues

- Produce a Monday morning weekly report using our exact format (provided)

- Respond to reader
s using approved templates

- Track all affiliate conversions across 8+ programs and reconcile against analytics



Monthly responsibilities:



- Hire and onboard new writers as we scale (2–4 writers to manage by month 3)

- Run monthly technical SEO audit

- Update and maintain the comparison pages and review articles as offers change

- Identify 3–5 new keyword opportunities from competitor analysis





WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE



Month 3:

- 30+ high-quality articles published

- 500+
 subscribers

- 10+ backlinks from DR 40+ sites



Month 6:

- 60+ articles ranking in top 50

- 3,000+
 subscribers

- First $5K+ month in affiliate revenue



Month 12:

- Site traffic above 50K monthly sessions

- 15,000+
 subscribers

- Monthly revenue above $30K



When we hit these milestones, your compensation scales. This is not a vague promise — we will put the specific triggers in writing in your contract.





HOW TO APPLY



Applications that do not follow this format will be ignored. We are looking for someone who reads carefully and follows instructions — prove that in your application.



Send us:



1. A 5-minute Loom video introducing yourself. Tell us: (a) your name, (b) one website you helped grow and the specific results you achieved, (c) why you think you are a fit for this role, (d) one thing you noticed about 
 that you would change immediately.



2. A written answer to these three questions (maximum 3 sentences each):

   • What is your current experience with YMYL or finance-niche SEO?

   • Walk us through how you would choose between publishing 5 long-form articles vs 15 short articles per month.

   • What is the difference between Goldco and Augusta Precious Metals, and which would you prioritize in a comparison article? (You are allowed to research — we want to see how you think, not what you already know.)



3. Links to 2–3 live websites you have worked on, with a one-sentence description of your contribution.



4. Your preferred start date and confirmation that you can commit to 8 hours per day, 5 days per week, for at least 6 months.



5. In your application subject line, write: "EAS-VA Application — [Your Name] — [Number of years of SEO experience]"



We review applications within 5 business days. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a 30-minute video interview, followed by a paid 5-day trial period at full rate.





FINAL NOTE



We are serious operators building a serious business. We value initiative, honesty, and follow-through over credentials. If you are hungry, fast, and take ownership — even if your resume is not perfect — we want to hear from you.



If you are looking for easy work, passive hours, or something you can do while managing five other clients, this is not the role for you.



We are hiring ONE person for this role. Take your time with your application.

title: Senior Operations & Client Success Lead
description:
Job Title:** GoHighLevel & Apollo Campaign Virtual Assistant (PH-Based, Remote)  



Type:** Full-time, Remote (Philippines)  

 Compensation Range:**  

- Trial month-to-month: 900–1,200 USD/month  

- Strong month-to-month: 1,200–1,500 USD/month  

- Long-term contract: 1,300–1,700 USD/month  

- Permanent hire with team-lead path: 1,600–2,200 USD/month  



About Alchemy Flow



Alchemy Flow builds AI-powered revenue systems for service businesses. A key part of our delivery is an AI voice agent that answers inbound calls, makes outbound calls, schedules appointments, handles FAQs, and coordinates with human tea
mbers when needed.  



This role owns the execution around that system: campaigns, data, CRM, and call workflows.



Role Summary



You will be the GoHighLevel and Apollo Campaign Virtual Assistant for Alchemy Flow. Your primary responsibilities are to pull and clean prospect lists, build and manage campaigns in GoHighLevel, and support AI voice workflows that handle inbound and outbound calls, appointment scheduling, live transfers, and FAQs.  



You will help ensure every call, campaign, and contact is handled accurately, logged properly, and moves prospects toward booked appointments and reactivations. As you grow, this role can develop into a team-lead position supervising other VAs.



Key Responsibilities



1. Campaign and Data Operations (Apollo + GoHighLevel)



- Pull and clean prospect lists from Apollo based on defined ICPs and campaign criteria.  

- Build and manage contact imports into GoHighLevel, including tagging, segmenting, and pipeline placement.  

- Launch and monitor outbound campaigns 
, SMS, and call workflows) tied to our AI voice agent and follow-up sequences.  

- Maintain CRM hygiene: update statuses, clean duplicates, track stages, and ensure accurate reporting.



2. Support AI Voice Agent Workflows



- Align campaigns with the AI voice agent workflow, which includes:  

  - Answering inbound calls within 1–2 rings with a consistent script.  

  - Identifying caller intent (new appointment, existing appointment, reschedule/cancel, emergency, FAQ, billing, specific person, general support).  

  - Supporting appointment scheduling flows by ensuring calendars, appointment types, and notifications are configured correctly.  

  - Enabling reschedule and cancellation paths that verify caller identity and protect the schedule.  

  - Maintaining an accurate FAQ knowledge base (business hours, location, services, basic pricing, what to expect, what to bring).  

- Ensure live-transfer rules are correctly implemented so urgent, sensitive, or complex calls are routed to the right human tea
mber.



3. Appointment and Follow-Up Support



- Assist with follow-up and appointment-setting workflows generated by the AI system (reminders, confirmations, reschedules, reactivations).  

- Support outbound use cases such as:  

  - Appointment reminders and follow-ups  

  - Reactivation campaigns  

  - Missed-call recovery  

  - Lead qualification callbacks  



4. Logging, QA, and Reporting Support



- Help ensure every call is logged with:  

  - Caller information  

  - Intent and outcome  

  - Whether an appointment was booked  

  - Whether a transfer occurred  

  - Notes for internal review  

- Flag issues, anomalies, or recurring problems in call logs and campaigns so they can be improved.  

- Document repeatable processes and help refine SOPs for voice workflows and campaigns.



5. Client and Internal Communication



- Support client communication when needed (status updates, simple clarification questions, basic reporting).  

- Escalate issues clearly and professionally to the founder or other tea
mbers.  

- As the role grows, potentially join occasional client-facing calls to walk through campaign or CRM details.



 Ideal Candidate Profile



**Location & Schedule**



- Based in the Philippines.  

- Able to work with some overlap in US business hours.



Experience



- Proven experience as a virtual assistant or operations/campaign VA (agency, SaaS, or service business experience is a plus).  

- Hands-on experience with:  

  - Apollo (or similar tools) for list building and prospect research.  

  - GoHighLevel (or similar CRMs) for workflows, pipeline management, and campaign setup.



Skills and Attributes



- Strong written English; clear, concise communication.  

- Good verbal communication for occasional client-facing support.

title: Local SEO and Ai search optimization
description:
SPA MARKETING

JOB POSTING — REMOTE POSITION



Local SEO & AI Search Optimization Specialist

Remote • Full-Time or Contract • Immediate Start



About the Role

SPA Marketing is a growing digital marketing agency specializing in helping local businesses dominate their markets online. We work with clients across a range of industries — from health & wellness to home services — and we're looking for a sharp, results-driven SEO specialist who lives and breathes local search and understands how AI is reshaping the way people discover businesses online.

This is an online, fully remote position. You'll work closely with our strategy team to build and execute local SEO campaigns, optimize for AI-driven search experiences (including Google's AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and similar platforms), and report measurable results to clients. If you know your way around Google Business Profile, citation building, schema markup, and you're genuinely excited about how large language models are changing the search landscape, we want to hear from you.



What You'll Do



Develop and execute local SEO strategies for clients in competitive markets

Optimize Google Business Profiles, manage reviews strategy, and oversee local citation building and cleanup

Conduct keyword research with a focus on local intent, near-me queries, and conversational/voice search patterns

Implement structured data (schema markup) to improve visibility in rich results and AI-generated summaries

Monitor and adapt strategies for AI search surfaces including Google AI Overviews, SGE, Bing Copilot, and LLM-based discovery platforms

Audit and improve on-page SEO, internal linking, and content structure for local relevance

Build and manage location pages, service area content, and geo-targeted landing pages

Track and report on rankings, local pack visibility, GBP insights, organic traffic, and lead generation KPIs

Stay current on Google algorithm updates and evolving AI search behaviors

Collaborate with our content and paid media teams for integrated campaign execution





What We're Looking For

Required Skills & Experience



2+ years of hands-on local SEO experience with demonstrated client results

Deep familiarity with Google Business Profile management and optimization

Experience with citation management platforms (BrightLocal, Whitespark, Yext, or similar)

Strong understanding of technical SEO: schema markup, crawl optimization, Core Web Vitals

Working knowledge of how AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews) surface local results

Proficiency with Google Search Console, GA4, SEMrush, Ahrefs, or Moz

Strong written communication skills — you'll be writing client-facing reports and strategy docs

Ability to manage multiple client accounts simultaneously with strong organizational skills



Bonus Points For



Experience optimizing content to appear in LLM-generated answers (answer engine optimization / AEO)

Familiarity with prompt engineering or AI content workflows

Experience in multi-location SEO or franchise-level campaigns

Understanding of the relationship between local SEO and paid local search (LSAs, Google Ads)

Any experience with reputation management platforms





Our Hiring Process

We receive a high volume of applications for every role we post, and frankly, most of them tell us very little about whether someone will actually perform. Generic cover letters and resume keywords only go so far. What we care about is whether you actually read, pay attention to detail, and can think clearly about SEO — three things that matter enormously in this job.

To make sure we're spending our time on the right candidates, we use a simple filter. Before you apply through any job board, please send a brief
 to 
 with the subject line: "AI Local SEO — I Actually Read This" and in the body, answer the following question in 3–5 sentences: What is one concrete way a local business can improve its chances of being cited or recommended by an AI search engine like ChatGPT or Google's AI Overviews — and why does that tactic work? Applications that skip this step will not be reviewed. This is not optional and it is not a formality — it's the first real test of whether you're a good fit for this team.

Once we receive your
, our team will follow up within 3–5 business days with next steps, which typically include a short async video intro and a paid audit task on a sample client account.



Access to premium SEO and AI tools (we cover your stack)

Ongoing professional development budget for courses, conferences, and certifications

Supportive, small-team culture where your ideas actually get implemented

Performance bonuses tied to client retention and campaign results

title: Dedicated HubSpot Implementation Developer (Long-term partnership with an Agency)
description:
Summary:



I’m looking for a hands-on HubSpot implementation developer to work with me on an ongoing basis.



This is not a strategy or consulting role.

Your core job is to take clearly scoped tasks, ask thoughtful clarification questions when needed, and execute them cleanly in HubSpot.



You’ll be working directly with me. I design the systems and logic. You build them correctly.



This role suits someone who has already built HubSpot environments from scratch, understands how different hubs interact, and can move fast without breaking data integrity.



What You’ll Be Doing (Core Responsibilities)

• Building and maintaining HubSpot workflows (Sales, Marketing & Operations)

• Creating and managing:

• Custom properties (with correct data types & logic)

• Deal, Contact, Company & Custom Object automations

• Lead routing & ownership logic

• Lifecycle stage & pipeline logic

• Implementing reporting & dashboards:

• Sales performance

• Funnel conversion

• Pipeline health

• Activity & productivity tracking

• Working across all HubSpot hubs:

• Sales Hub

• Marketing Hub

• Operations Hub

• Service Hub (where relevant)

• Supporting integrations and data flows (Stripe, forms, external tools where required)

• Executing daily task lists with minimal hand-holding





How We’ll Work Together

I’ll give you:

• Clear tasks

• Context on why something is being built

You’ll:

• Ask clarification questions once

• Confirm understanding

• Execute accurately

Expect:

• Structured work

• High standards

• Long-term collaboration if you perform well





Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)



You must have:

• Proven experience building HubSpot systems, not just using them

• Strong understanding of:

• Workflow logic

• Re-enrolment criteria

• Property syncing & precedence

• Data hygiene

• Experience working with:

• Multiple pipelines

• Complex lifecycle logic

• Custom objects

• Confidence working independently once requirements are clear



If you’ve only done light CRM admin work or basic
 workflows, this role is not for you.



Nice to Have (Bonus)

• Experience with:

• Stripe / payments logic

• Deal-to-invoice style flows

• Reporting for leadership / revenue tracking

• Familiarity with automation-first system design

• Comfort working with structured SOPs and logic diagrams





Application Requirements



To apply, you must include:

1. A brief summary of your HubSpot implementation experience

2. Examples of:

• Automations you’ve built

• Reports or dashboards you’ve implemented

• Any complex workflows you’re proud of

3. Confirmation that you’re comfortable completing a short practical task as part of the selection process



Applications without relevant HubSpot experience will be ignored.



Engagement Details

• Ongoing work (not a one-off project)

• Flexible hours

• Paid hourly or retainer (to be discussed)

• Long-term opportunity for the right person

title: Ad Translator / Localizer
description:

 + ElevenLabs) – Part-Time / Long Term

The Job: You will produce 6–10 video variations per day. You do not need to film or create videos from scratch. Your job is to take existing winning ads and localize them for new markets using AI tools.

Your Daily Workflow:



You will be given video's to

Translate & Sub: Use 
 to auto-generate translated subtitles.

Dub: Use ElevenLabs to create a localized AI voiceover.

Edit: Sync the audio and text so it looks like a native ad.



Requirements:



Experience with 
 and ElevenLabs is a big plus (but we will train you).

A good feel for languages is a strong bonus — you don't need to be fluent in the target languages (AI handles translation), but being able to spot when a subtitle, dub, or phrasing feels "off" is a huge asset. (Most common languages are German, French and Dutch, but can be any EU language) 



If you speak or have studied more than one language, tell us.

Ability to hit the daily target (6–10 videos) consistently.

Attention to detail (syncing audio dubs perfectly with video).

 and ElevenLabs subscriptions.

Good luck,

Nelson

description:

We're looking for a creative ad producer to design and edit short-form video ads for our

title: Customer Support Manager for Shopify Fashion Ecommerce (Part-Time, Long-Term, Remote)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



This is a long-term part-time role for an experienced Customer Support Manager. We are not looking for someone for one month. We want someone we can grow with, give more responsibility to, and pay more over time as the team scales.



You will NOT be answering tickets. You will be managing the people who do.



We already have a clear structure in place: written SOPs, proven support funnels, macros, and defined workflows that we continue to optimize. We are NOT hiring you to redesign or rebuild our customer support process. We are hiring you to make sure the structure we have is followed consistently, every day, by every CSR on the team.



Your job is to make sure our customer support team handles every ticket according to the SOPs and macros, our refund rate stays low, and our KPIs stay healthy. You will audit ticket samples daily, track team performance against our SOP, write coaching feedback, and report weekly to the company directors with a clear summary of what is working, what is not, and what needs to change.



You will be managing a team of 3 to 4 CSRs. Part of your role is active two-way communication: coaching and feedback downward to the CSRs when they deviate from SOP, and clear reporting upward to the directors on team performance (FRT, Resolution Time, Refund Rate, SOP compliance) and where the biggest opportunities for improvement sit. You are the link between the team and leadership.



-------------------------



WHAT YOU WILL DO DAILY (2 to 3 hours per day, 7 days per week)



- Audit a sample of Gorgias tickets handled by our 3 to 4 CSRs against our SOP and macros

- Score CSRs on SOP compliance, tone, refund handling, and escalation judgment

- Write short coaching notes when CSRs deviate from SOP, and follow up to confirm the behavior changed

- Track KPIs: First Response Time (FRT), Resolution Time, Refund Rate, SOP compliance percentage

- Communicate directly with CSRs on performance, corrections, and questions about the workflow

- Run the weekly Risky Products Report process (every Monday before 12:00 PHT), chasing CS and Disputes for input and escalating to the product team

- Send a weekly KPI and insights report to the company directors, highlighting team performance and where the opportunities for improvement sit

- Flag patterns: which products drive refunds, which CSRs need coaching, where the SOP has gaps



-------------------------



WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT



- This is not a role to rebuild or redesign the customer support process. The structure, funnels, SOPs, and macros are already in place and working.

- This is not a ticket-handling role. If you are looking for hands-on CSR work, this is not a fit.

- This is not a role for someone who wants to "make exceptions to be nice." We enforce process.



-------------------------



SCHEDULE. READ THIS BEFORE APPLYING



This role is 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day. Customer support runs every day, so quality oversight runs every day. If you cannot commit to working 7 days a week, please do not apply. We will not make exceptions, and applying anyway only wastes both our time. You can choose your hours within the day, but you must check in 7 days a week.



-------------------------



WHO YOU ARE



- You have hands-on, provable experience MANAGING a customer support team in ecommerce. Not just doing the work yourself, but auditing others, coaching, and reporting up. Direct CSM experience, OR senior CS team lead experience, OR CS QA / Quality Analyst experience all count (any combination works). Be prepared to show concrete examples in round 2.

- You have used Gorgias before. This is not negotiable for this role.

- You have worked with Shopify ecommerce stores.

- Bonus: experience in fashion ecommerce specifically.

- You are comfortable working with numbers. Pivot tables, percentages, refund rates, KPI dashboards. You do not need to be an analyst, but you cannot be afraid of a spreadsheet.

- Your written English is professional and precise. You can write coaching feedback that is direct without being rude, and reports that a busy executive can read in two minutes.

- You are a process person. You believe SOPs exist for a reason and you enforce them.



-------------------------



WHAT WE OFFER



- $6 to $8 USD per hour base, depending on proven experience

- Long-term role. We want to grow with the right person and increase pay and responsibility over time

- A fully built support operation: written SOPs, macros, proven funnels, and a defined workflow. You will not be guessing what the standards are, and you will not be asked to build them from scratch

- A direct line to the company directors. Your reports go straight up, no middle layer



-------------------------



HOW TO APPLY. READ CAREFULLY



We will only review applications that follow these instructions exactly. This is the first thing we evaluate.



1. Put the word PRECISION in the subject line of your application.



2. In your message, answer all four of these questions inline (not in an attached file):



   Q1. How long have you been managing or auditing a customer support team in ecommerce, and at what kind of company (size, niche, ticket volume, number of CSRs you oversaw)? Name the actual employer if you can.



   Q2. Describe one specific time you identified that a customer support agent was handling tickets incorrectly. What was the issue, how did you find it, how did you coach the agent, and did the behavior actually change afterwards?



   Q3. Have you used Gorgias? For how long, and at what ticket volume? If yes, name one Gorgias feature you use regularly that most people do not know about.



   Q4. Confirm you can commit to 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day, long-term. List the working hours you would propose in your local timezone.



3. State your hourly rate in USD.



Applications missing the keyword PRECISION, or that skip any of the four questions, will not be reviewed. Loom videos, CVs, and portfolio links are not needed at this stage. Please do not send them. We will request them in round 2 from shortlisted candidates.

title: CS Team Lead / QA Manager for Shopify Ecommerce (Part-Time, Remote, Long-Term)
description:
THE ROLE



Part-time Customer Support Manager to oversee a small team of 3 to 4 CSRs in our Shopify fashion ecommerce business. You will not answer tickets. You will make sure the people who do, do it correctly.



Our SOPs, macros, and support funnels are already built and working. We need someone to enforce them: audit tickets daily, coach CSRs, track KPIs (FRT, Resolution Time, Refund Rate, SOP compliance), and report weekly to the directors.



This is not a role to redesign our CS process. It is a role to protect the process we already have.



-------------------------



DAILY (2 to 3 hours per day, 7 days per week)



- Audit a sample of Gorgias tickets against our SOP

- Coach CSRs when they deviate from process, follow up to check the behavior changed

- Track KPIs and run the weekly Risky Products Report (every Monday, 12:00 PHT)

- Weekly KPI and insights report to the company directors



-------------------------



7 DAYS A WEEK. READ THIS



This role is 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day. If you cannot do 7 days, please do not apply. No exceptions.



-------------------------



REQUIREMENTS



- Proven experience managing or QA-auditing a CS team in ecommerce (be ready to show examples)

- Hands-on Gorgias experience, not negotiable

- Shopify ecommerce background

- Comfortable with spreadsheets and KPI numbers

- Clear, precise written English (coaching notes and executive reports)

- Process-driven. You enforce SOPs, you do not make exceptions



Bonus: fashion ecommerce experience.



-------------------------



PAY



$6 to $8 USD per hour, based on proven experience. Long-term, with pay and responsibility growing as the team scales.



-------------------------



HOW TO APPLY



We only review applications that follow these instructions exactly.



1. Put the word PRECISION in the subject line.



2. Answer all four questions inline in your message (no attachments):



   Q1. How long have you managed or audited a CS team in ecommerce? What company, what niche, what ticket volume, and how many CSRs did you oversee? Name the employer if possible.



   Q2. Describe one specific time you caught a CSR handling tickets incorrectly. How did you find it, how did you coach them, and did the behavior change?



   Q3. How long have you used Gorgias, and at what ticket volume? Name one Gorgias feature most people do not know about.



   Q4. Confirm you can commit to 7 days a week, 2 to 3 hours per day, long-term. List your proposed working hours in your local timezone.



3. State your hourly rate in USD.



Missing the PRECISION keyword or skipping questions = we do not review. No Loom, CV, or portfolio needed yet. We request those in round 2.

title: Copy Director
description:
Circle of Greatness is seeking a highly strategic and performance-driven Copy Director to lead all copy across our
, SMS, funnel, and website marketing ecosystem.



This role is responsible for owning messaging across the entire customer journey — from first touchpoint to conversion. You will manage and mentor junior copywriters, collaborate directly with partners, and ensure every piece of communication aligns with both brand voice and revenue goals.



The ideal candidate is both creative and analytical — someone who understands how to craft compelling messaging and optimize it across funnels, campaigns, and web experiences.



Key Responsibilities

Copy Leadership & Strategy



Own and oversee all copy across
, SMS, funnels, and website pages

Define messaging frameworks, campaign angles, and conversion strategies

Guide and develop junior copywriters on tone, structure, and execution

Set clear standards for what “high-performing” copy looks like





Quality Control



Review, edit, and approve all outgoing copy across campaigns and web assets

Ensure consistency in brand voice across all platforms and partners

Maintain a high bar for clarity, persuasion, and conversion effectiveness





Performance & Optimization



Analyze performance across campaigns and funnels (open rates, CTR, conversions, revenue)

Identify winning messaging patterns and scale them

Continuously test, iterate, and optimize copy across
, SMS, and web pages

Use data to inform creative and strategic decisions





Client & Partner Management



Act as the primary copy lead for 4–5 key partners

Collaborate on campaign, funnel, and offer strategy

Ensure all deliverables align with partner goals and expectations





Campaign & Funnel Execution



Work closely with junior copywriters to produce high-quality campaigns and funnel assets

Oversee copy for landing pages, sales pages, and key website touchpoints

Support launches for promotions, events, and marketing initiatives

Ensure all copy is delivered accurately and on time





Requirements



Proven experience in copywriting and managing copy teams, preferably in digital marketing

Strong expertise in
, SMS, funnel, and website copywriting

Track record of writing or directing copy that drives measurable results (revenue, conversions)

Experience mentoring or managing junior tea
mbers

Ability to balance creative direction with data-driven decision-making

Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

High attention to detail and commitment to quality





Nice to Have



Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth or entrepreneurial environments

Experience managing multiple brands or partners simultaneously

Familiarity with tools like GoHighLevel, Klaviyo, Postscript, Attentive, or similar platforms

Understanding of direct response marketing and funnel strategy



What Success Looks Like



Campaigns and funnels consistently improve in open rates, CTR, conversions, and revenue

Website and funnel copy clearly communicates value and drives action

Messaging is consistent, compelling, and aligned across all channels

Junior copywriters are producing higher-quality, conversion-focused work

Copy becomes a core driver of revenue growth for Circle of Greatness





Apply

title: Senior Accountant (Australian Tax and Business Services)
description:
Australian firm | Full-time role



About Create Financial

Create Financial is a modern Australian tax and advisory firm founded in 2025, built for Australia’s innovation ecosystem.



We work with founders and high-growth businesses across their entire journey — from idea -> growth -> scale -> exit.



Our services span:

- Tax and compliance

- ASIC and company secretarial

- R&D Tax Incentive

- CFO and commercial advisory



The Opportunity

This is a newly created role to support our growing client base. You’ll work directly with the Managing Partner and get exposure well beyond a traditional accounting firm role.



This role is ideal for a newly qualified CA or CPA coming out of an Australian business services or tax environment who wants something more flexible, commercial, and interesting.



It’s particularly well suited to someone returning to the workforce (e.g. a parent) who still wants to do high-quality work — without the rigid structure of a traditional firm.



You’ll get exposure to startups, founders, and real commercial decision-making — not just compliance work.



What You’ll Be Doing

You’ll support both client delivery and internal operations across the firm, including:



Client Accounting & Compliance

- Month-end support and financial reporting for clients

- Bank, payroll, and balance sheet reconciliations (Xero)

- Preparation of BAS, IAS, and income tax returns

- Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT) returns



Advisory & Operations Support

- Assist with entity setup and structuring

- Prepare ASIC forms and support ongoing compliance

- Support CFO/advisory engagements (cash flow, reporting, insights)



Growth & Learning

- Assist with R&D Tax Incentive claims and documentation

- Work with startups and scale-ups across different industries

- Exposure to grants, funding, and investor readiness



What We’re Looking For

- CA or CPA qualified (or very close to completion)

- 3–6 years’ experience in Australian business services / tax

- Strong working knowledge of Xero (non-negotiable)

- Experience preparing BAS, tax returns, and reconciliations

- Comfortable working directly with clients



How You Work

- Detail-oriented but pragmatic

- Comfortable in a fast-moving, less structured environment

- Proactive and able to manage your own time

- Enjoy working closely with founders and small teams

- Curious — you want to learn beyond compliance



Why Join Create Financial

- Flexibility first — part-time hours that fit around your life

- Remote-friendly — work from anywhere

- High-impact work — direct exposure to founders and decision-making

- Variety — no two clients or days are the same

- Growth — learn CFO, advisory, and R&D (not just tax)

- Small practice — no bureaucracy, real ownership

title: Scribe for Mental Health Practice
description:
Job Title: Full-Time Virtual Medical Scribe & Administrative Assistant (Philippines-Based)

We are seeking a dedicated, full-time Virtual Medical Scribe / Administrative Assistant based in the Philippines to support a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner (PMHNP) in a busy U.S. practice.

Position Overview?This is a full-time remote position focused primarily on medical scribing for psychiatric and mental health patient encounters. You will use Freed AI (ambient AI scribe technology) and the Tebra EHR/practice management platform to create accurate, timely clinical documentation. Additional administrative support will be required as needed, where a clear, professional American English accent is a strong advantage.

Key Responsibilities

	•	Listen to and scribe patient sessions in real-time or shortly after using Freed AI to generate detailed clinical notes (SOAP notes, progress notes, psychiatric evaluations, etc.).

	•	Review, edit, and finalize documentation directly in the Tebra platform.

	•	Ensure all notes are accurate, compliant with HIPAA, and completed promptly.

	•	Handle additional administrative duties as assigned, such as scheduling, patient communications, phone calls, follow-ups, prior authorizations, or other office tasks.

	•	Collaborate closely with the PMHNP via virtual tools (Zoom, messaging platforms, etc.).

Requirements

	•	Based in the Philippines with reliable high-speed internet and a quiet, professional workspace.

	•	Proven experience as a medical scribe, virtual medical assistant, or in clinical documentation/transcription (mental health or psychiatry experience is a big plus).

	•	Strong, hands-on familiarity with Freed AI and Tebra is required — you must be comfortable using both tools daily.

	•	Excellent written and spoken English, with strong attention to detail and solid knowledge of medical terminology (especially psychiatric/mental health terms).

	•	Clear, professional American English accent is highly preferred for phone calls and other voice-based administrative duties.

	•	Full-time availability (approximately 40 hours/week) aligned with Alaska time zone business hours (typically evening/night shifts in the Philippines).

	•	Professional, reliable, proactive, and able to work independently while maintaining patient confidentiality.

	•	Computer setup suitable for healthcare documentation.

What We Offer

	•	Full-time, long-term position with consistent hours.

	•	Competitive compensation based on experience.

	•	Opportunity to support meaningful mental health care using advanced AI tools.

	•	Training and ongoing support provided.

If you are detail-oriented, tech-savvy, and experienced with Freed AI and Tebra, we would love to hear from you!

To Apply:?Please send your resume, a short cover letter highlighting your experience with Freed AI and Tebra, and a brief voice sample (30–60 seconds) demonstrating your American English accent.
salary: $6.00 HR and Up Depending Upon Experienc

description:

Explicit language

Sexually explicit or suggestive content

Politically sensitive themes

Sound effects and non-music elements

Intros, interludes, skits, and outros

Silent sections or “dead space”

Ensure clean, consistent audio output

Export/encode files into specified codecs and formats

Follow detailed editing and naming conventions

Maintain organized workflow across multiple files

Strong English communication skills (written & spoken)

Ability to follow detailed instructions and guidelines

Strong attention to detail and consistency

Reliable internet connection

Availability during U.S. daytime hours

Performance-based pay after training

Opportunity to grow into a consistent part-time role

Increased earning potential based on speed and accuracy



Ideal Candidate:



Highly detail-oriented and quality-focused

Able to process repetitive tasks efficiently

Comfortable working independently

Quick learner with strong organizational skills

Experience with batch processing workflows is a plus

title: Chief of Staff to CEO – Execution and Leverage (Remote)
description:
Chief of Staff to CEO – Execution and Leverage (Remote)



Aureus Financial is scaling at over 100% year on year across nine business divisions. We are building toward a $100M group and are seeking a Chief of Staff to act as the CEO’s execution partner during this next phase of growth.



This is not a traditional Executive Assistant role. You will be taking over from a task-oriented EA model and stepping into a significantly higher-impact position. The expectation is ownership, judgment, and execution leadership.



*Do not apply if you cannot provide proof of at least 5 years experience in similar roles - unqualified applications will be ignored*



You will run the CEO’s inbox and calendar because that is where priorities are created or destroyed. But your real job is to convert strategy into shipped outcomes, create clarity across teams, and ensure nothing critical stalls due to lack of structure or follow-through.



If you have at least 5 years experience working closely with fast-paced founders or executives in high-growth environments, you will recognise the pace and responsibility immediately. If you are looking for a predictable, task-driven support role, this will not be a fit.



What You Will Own

CEO inbox and calendar as strategic tools. Execution cadence for CEO priorities. Project tracking, accountability, and delivery across divisions. Systems that create visibility and reduce friction. Follow-through on strategic initiatives and commitments.



How Success Is Measured

CEO trust and satisfaction at 9/10 or higher. CEO priorities moving forward without repeated follow-up. Increased CEO focus on strategy and growth. Faster execution and cleaner accountability across teams.



You Are Likely a Fit If

You have operated in a high-growth business. You think in systems and frameworks. You communicate clearly and decisively. You are comfortable pushing back and making judgment calls. You care deeply about outcomes and execution quality.



You Are Not a Fit If

You identify primarily as a task-based EA. You need detailed instructions to take action. You avoid accountability or difficult conversations. You prefer slow-moving, highly structured environments.



How to Apply



Review the job post - 



Submit your CV and a 2-3 minute Loom introducing yourself and explaining: What does an A+ week look like in this role, and how would you run the CEO office in your first 30 days?



Do not apply via OnlineJobs.



Applicants who do not complete the application requirements or send a generic application will not be considered.

title: Full-Stack Developer (Next.js/Supabase) – AI-Augmented Development & CRM Feature Expansion
description:
I am looking for a skilled Full-Stack Developer to continue the development of iOpt_CRM, a custom CRM solution. This project was built using Claude Code (Anthropic’s CLI tool), and I am looking for a developer who is comfortable working with AI tools to maintain and expand the codebase rather than performing a manual rewrite.



I value speed and efficiency. This project utilizes AI for rapid prototyping and feature deployment. Your role is to act as the "pilot"—using AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot) to generate code, while providing the expert oversight to ensure security, performance, and logical consistency.



Key Responsibilities

Feature Development: Build out new modules (Leads, Pipeline management, Reporting) within the existing framework.



AI Orchestration: Use AI coding assistants to accelerate development while ensuring the output matches the existing project structure.



Database Management: Manage and optimize Supabase (PostgreSQL) schemas, Row Level Security (RLS), and edge functions.



UI/UX: Implement clean, responsive interfaces using Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui.



Code Quality: Review AI-generated code for technical debt and ensure it integrates seamlessly with the current architecture.



Required Skills

Frameworks: Next.js (App Router), React, and TypeScript.



Backend/Auth: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage).



Styling: Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components.



Tooling: Experience with Claude Code, Cursor, or deep proficiency in prompting AI for complex coding tasks.



Git: Strong version control habits.

title: PPC Specialist – Amazon Marketing Agency
description:
PPC Specialist – Amazon Marketing Agency (Remote)



Full-Time | Remote | Competitive Salary + PTO



About Us:



The Amazon Whisperer is a fast-growing, full-service Amazon marketing agency that delivers profitable sales growth for consumer brands. We specialize in Amazon SEO, PPC, listing optimization, and catalog management, and we do it with systems, strategy, and speed. We’re looking for a PPC Specialist who wants to grow into a world-class media buyer by executing and managing high-performing Amazon ad campaigns.



The Role:



The PPC Specialist plays a hands-on role in the day-to-day execution of Amazon advertising campaigns. You’ll be responsible for uploading bulk files, adjusting bids and budgets, building campaigns, harvesting keywords, and ensuring PPC performance stays aligned with ACoS, TACoS, and budget targets. This is a perfect fit for someone who wants to master the technical side of Amazon ads and learn how to run efficient, scalable campaigns from the ground up.



You’ll work directly under the PPC Lead, taking direction on strategy while owning the implementation across multiple client accounts.



Responsibilities:



- Work closely with the PPC Lead to implement strategies across client accounts

- Create new campaigns (SP, SB, SD) and restructure existing ones for efficiency

- Adjust bids, update budgets, and modify placement settings

- Harvest high-performing keywords and promote them into new campaigns

- Apply negative keyword targeting to eliminate wasted ad spend

- Track and maintain campaign health daily to ensure accounts remain within budget

- Monitor and flag issues with ACoS, TACoS, or unexpected spend spikes

- Harvest PPC data to support organic SEO initiatives

- Maintain clean data and reports in spreadsheets and tracking tools



Requirements:



- Minimum 2 years of Amazon PPC experience (SEO knowledge is a plus)

- Proficiency in Helium 10, Data Dive, Jungle Scout, or similar keyword tools

- Strong analytical mindset and comfort working with performance data

- Must be proficient in Google Sheets and Excel

- Experience using automated ad platforms like Trellis, Adlabs, Bidx, Teikametrics, Quartile, Perpetua, or other similar sofware

- Clear communication skills for working with team leads and managers

- Reliable and organized, able to manage multiple campaigns without hand-holding

- Fast and reliable internet connection

- Fluent English (written and spoken)

- Ability to type 55+ words per minute



Company Culture:



At The Amazon Whisperer, we highly value transparency, communication, and trust. This is not just another job, it’s a place where your ideas are valued, hard work is rewarded, and growth opportunities are everywhere. We’re building a team that operates like a unit. Supportive, proactive, and relentlessly focused on client success. If you’re looking for a long-term remote role where you can grow your skills and career, you’re in the right place.



What You’ll Get:



- Competitive pay

- 13 days PTO (including public holidays)

- 100% remote work

- Opportunity to grow into roles like PPC Lead and beyond

- A collaborative and growth-minded team environment



Apply today and be part of a company that’s scaling fast and doing things differently in the Amazon space. We’re not looking for average. We’re looking for exceptional.



Submit your application here:

The Amazon Whisperer is a fast-growing, full-service Amazon marketing agency focused on delivering profitable sales growth for consumer brands on the world's largest marketplace. We specialize in Amazon SEO, PPC, listing optimization, and catalog management — and we're building out the sales engine to match our 
ntum.



We're looking for a sharp, hungry SDR who knows Amazon inside and out and can turn cold prospects into qualified, booked meetings with confidence.



The Role As an SDR at The Amazon Whisperer, you'll be on the front lines of our outbound sales operation. You'll work a mix of cold outreach and warm lead follow-up — researching prospects, identifying pain points, and booking qualified discovery calls for our closing team. This isn't a script-reading role. You'll need to actually understand Amazon, speak a seller's language, and earn the meeting.



Core Responsibilities:

Outbound Dialing & Prospecting: Work structured lead lists through cold calling and warm follow-up sequences

Reach out to inbound leads from lead magnets,
 campaigns, and other marketing channels

Execute pre-call warm-up sequences via
 and LinkedIn before dialing

Lead Research & Qualification: Research prospects directly in Amazon Seller Central to identify performance gaps, listing issues, and growth opportunities

Qualify leads on the call against defined criteria before booking meetings

Ensure only high-quality, sales-ready leads reach the closing team

Appointment Setting: Book qualified discovery calls with brand owners, founders, and marketing decision-makers

Own show rates — follow up, confirm, and reduce no-shows

title: Amazon SEO / Cataloging Specialist
description:
SEO Cataloging Specialist – Amazon Marketing Agency (Remote)



Full-Time | Remote | Competitive Salary + PTO



About Us:



The Amazon Whisperer is a fast-growing, full-service Amazon marketing agency that delivers profitable sales growth for consumer brands. We specialize in Amazon SEO, PPC, listing optimization, and catalog management, and we do it with systems, strategy, and speed. We’re looking for a highly organized and analytical SEO Cataloging Specialist to join our team and help power the backend of our clients’ Amazon success.



The Role:



The SEO Cataloging Specialist plays a critical dual role in our agency, driving traffic through SEO and keeping our clients’ Amazon catalogs healthy and optimized. You’ll be responsible for conducting in-depth keyword research, writing listing copy, updating keyword lists, and harvesting terms from PPC to enhance organic rankings. On the cataloging side, you’ll manage suppressed listings, submit reimbursements, upload images, and resolve backend issues with Amazon Seller Support.



This is a hands-on, detail-oriented role for someone who loves data, knows their way around the Amazon backend, and thrives in a fast-paced, remote environment.



Responsibilities:



- Conduct Amazon SEO research and build keyword strategies using tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, and Jungle Scout

- Update and maintain Master Keyword Lists (MKLs)

- Write Amazon listing copy including titles, bullets, descriptions, and backend search terms

- Harvest high-performing keywords from PPC campaigns to integrate into organic strategy

- Track and report on organic keyword ranking performance

- Troubleshoot suppressed listings, stranded inventory, and other catalog issues

- Submit FBA reimbursement claims and follow up as needed

- Manage catalog backend tasks such as uploading images, scheduling promotions, and opening cases with Amazon Seller Support

- Maintain accurate data in tracking sheets and ensure catalog compliance across accounts



Requirements:



- Minimum 2 years of Amazon experience working in catalog management and SEO optimization

- Proficiency with Helium 10, Data Dive, Jungle Scout, and other keyword tools

- Strong copywriting skills and ability to write SEO-friendly listing content

- Fluent English (both written and spoken)

- Extremely organized, reliable, and comfortable working in a fast paced environment

- Ability to type 55+ words per minute

- Fast and reliable internet connection

- Comfort using Google Sheets, Excel, and AI tools like ChatGPT



Company Culture:



At The Amazon Whisperer, we highly value transparency, communication, and trust. This is not just another job, it’s a place where your ideas are valued, hard work is rewarded, and growth opportunities are everywhere. We’re building a team that operates like a unit. Supportive, proactive, and relentlessly focused on client success. If you’re looking for a long-term remote role where you can grow your skills and career, you’re in the right place.



What You’ll Get:



- Competitive pay

- 13 days PTO (including public holidays)

- 100% remote work

- Room to grow into roles like Project Manager, SEO Lead, and beyond

- A collaborative and growth-minded team environment



Apply today and be part of a company that’s scaling fast and doing things differently in the Amazon space. We’re not looking for average. We’re looking for exceptional.



Submit your application here:

-
/
 relationships with project founders or marketing heads

YOU DON’T NEED:

THE ROLE:

 handle

title: Part-Time Content Creator + Admin Support
